````typescript
24:     <Popover>
25:       <PopoverTrigger asChild>
26:         <Button
27:           variant={"outline"}
28:           className={cn(
29:             "w-full justify-start text-left font-normal h-8 px-2.5 text-xs bg-background rounded-none",
30:             !date && "text-muted-foreground"
31:           )}
32:         >
33:           <CalendarIcon className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
34:           {date ? format(date, "PPP") : <span>{placeholder}</span>}
35:         </Button>
36:       </PopoverTrigger>
37:       <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-0">
38:         <Calendar
39:           mode="single"
40:           selected={date}
41:           onSelect={setDate}
42:           initialFocus
43:         />
44:       </PopoverContent>
45:     </Popover>
46:   )
47: }
````

## File: frontend/src/components/ui/multi-select.tsx
````typescript
  1: "use client"
  2: import * as React from "react"
  3: import { Check, X } from "lucide-react"
  4: import { CaretDownIcon } from "@phosphor-icons/react"
  5: 
  6: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
  7: import {
  8:   Command,
  9:   CommandEmpty,
 10:   CommandGroup,
 11:   CommandInput,
 12:   CommandItem,
 13:   CommandList,
 14: } from "@/components/ui/command"
 15: import {
 16:   Popover,
 17:   PopoverContent,
 18:   PopoverTrigger,
 19: } from "@/components/ui/popover"
 20: import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"
 21: 
 22: export type Option = {
 23:   label: string
 24:   value: string
 25: }
 26: 
 27: interface MultiSelectProps {
 28:   options: Option[]
 29:   selected: string[]
 30:   onChange: (values: string[]) => void
 31:   placeholder?: string
 32:   badgeClassName?: string
 33:   className?: string
 34: }
 35: 
 36: export function MultiSelect({ 
 37:   options, 
 38:   selected, 
 39:   onChange, 
 40:   placeholder = "Select...",
 41:   badgeClassName,
 42:   className,
 43: }: MultiSelectProps) {
 44:   const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(false)
 45: 
 46:   const handleUnselect = (item: string) => {
 47:     onChange(selected.filter((i) => i !== item))
 48:   }
 49: 
 50:   return (
 51:     <Popover open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
 52:       <PopoverTrigger asChild>
 53:         <div
 54:           role="combobox"
 55:           aria-expanded={open}
 56:           className={cn(
 57:             "flex w-full items-center justify-between gap-1.5 rounded-none border border-input bg-background py-1 pr-2 pl-2.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap transition-colors outline-none select-none",
 58:             "min-h-8 cursor-pointer",
 59:             "focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring/50",
 60:             "disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50",
 61:             "data-placeholder:text-muted-foreground",
 62:             className
 63:           )}
 64:         >
 65:           <div className="flex gap-1 flex-wrap">
 66:             {(!selected || selected.length === 0) && (
 67:               <span className="text-muted-foreground">{placeholder}</span>
 68:             )}
 69:             {selected?.map((item) => (
 70:               <Badge
 71:                 variant="secondary"
 72:                 key={item}
 73:                 className={cn("font-normal", badgeClassName)}
 74:                 onClick={(e) => {
 75:                   e.stopPropagation()
 76:                   handleUnselect(item)
 77:                 }}
 78:               >
 79:                 {options.find((opt) => opt.value === item)?.label || item}
 80:                 <X className="ml-1 h-3 w-3 hover:text-destructive cursor-pointer shrink-0" />
 81:               </Badge>
 82:             ))}
 83:           </div>
 84:           <CaretDownIcon className="pointer-events-none size-4 shrink-0 text-muted-foreground" />
 85:         </div>
 86:       </PopoverTrigger>
 87:       <PopoverContent className="w-[var(--radix-popover-trigger-width)] p-0 bg-popover" align="start">
 88:         <Command>
 89:           <CommandInput placeholder="Поиск..." />
 90:           <CommandList>
 91:             <CommandEmpty>Ничего не найдено.</CommandEmpty>
 92:             <CommandGroup>
 93:               {options.map((option) => (
 94:                 <CommandItem
 95:                   key={option.value}
 96:                   onSelect={() => {
 97:                     onChange(
 98:                       selected?.includes(option.value)
 99:                         ? selected.filter((item) => item !== option.value)
100:                         : [...(selected ?? []), option.value]
101:                     )
102:                   }}
103:                 >
104:                   <Check
105:                     className={cn(
106:                       "mr-2 h-4 w-4 shrink-0",
107:                       selected?.includes(option.value) ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0"
108:                     )}
109:                   />
110:                   {option.label}
111:                 </CommandItem>
112:               ))}
113:             </CommandGroup>
114:           </CommandList>
115:         </Command>
116:       </PopoverContent>
117:     </Popover>
118:   )
119: }
````

## File: frontend/Dockerfile
````
 1: # Stage 1: Dependencies
 2: FROM node:22-alpine AS deps
 3: WORKDIR /app
 4: 
 5: RUN corepack enable pnpm
 6: 
 7: COPY package.json pnpm-lock.yaml ./
 8: RUN pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
 9: 
10: # Stage 2: Builder
11: FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
12: WORKDIR /app
13: 
14: RUN corepack enable pnpm
15: 
16: COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
17: COPY . .
18: 
19: ARG NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
20: ENV NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=$NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
21: ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
22: RUN pnpm run build
23: 
24: # Stage 3: Runner
25: FROM node:22-alpine AS runner
26: WORKDIR /app
27: 
28: ENV NODE_ENV=production
29: ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
30: 
31: RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs \
32:     && adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
33: 
34: COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
35: COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
36: COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
37: 
38: USER nextjs
39: 
40: EXPOSE 3000
41: 
42: ENV PORT=3000
43: ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
44: 
45: CMD ["node", "server.js"]
````

## File: .agent/workflows/start-again.md
````markdown
 1: ---
 2: description: It formalizes the current state by applying a version tag and performs a "Hard Reset" on the development environment.
 3: ---
 4: 
 5: Role: Version Manager
 6: 
 7: Step 1: Version Tagging
 8: Identify Version: Check the last tag: git describe --tags --abbrev=0.
 9: 
10: Ask the User: "Is this a **Release** (increment minor version, e.g., v0.1.0 -> v0.2.0) or a **Hotfix** (increment patch version, e.g., v0.2.0 -> v0.2.1)?"
11: **STOP and wait for the user's answer before proceeding to the next steps.**
12: 
13: Calculate Next Tag (v[Status].[Release].[Hotfix]) based on user's answer:
14: 
15: Release: Increment the middle digit and set the last digit to 0 (e.g., v0.1.0 -> v0.2.0).
16: 
17: Hotfix: Increment the last digit (e.g., v0.2.0 -> v0.2.1).
18: 
19: Apply: Create the tag: git tag -a vX.X.X -m "Finalized Release vX.X.X".
20: 
21: Step 2: Hard Reset of Development Branches
22: Reset Backend: >    * git checkout develop-backend
23: 
24: git reset --hard main
25: 
26: Reset Frontend:
27: 
28: git checkout develop-frontend
29: 
30: git reset --hard main
31: 
32: Warning: This will delete any uncommitted or divergent code in both development branches.
33: 
34: Step 3: Global Deployment
35: Push Main & Tags: git push origin main --tags.
36: 
37: Force Push Backend: git push origin develop-backend --force.
38: 
39: Force Push Frontend: git push origin develop-frontend --force.
40: 
41: Step 4: Completion Report
42: Confirm the new Version Tag.
43: 
44: Confirm that main, develop-backend, and develop-frontend are now perfectly synchronized across local and remote repositories.
````

````go
 1: package domain
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
 5: 	"github.com/google/uuid"
 6: )
 7: 
 8: // AccessTokenClaims — payload embedded in the JWT.
 9: type AccessTokenClaims struct {
10: 	UserID    int       `json:"sub"`
11: 	SessionID uuid.UUID `json:"jti"`
12: 	Role      UserRole  `json:"role"`
13: 	Email     string    `json:"email,omitempty"`
14: 	jwt.RegisteredClaims
15: }
````

````go
 9: 	HashToken(raw string) string
````

## File: backend/internal/handler/dto/admin_employee_dto.go
````go
 1: package dto
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"encoding/json"
 5: 	"time"
 6: )
 7: 
 8: // PatchEmployeeProfileRequest is the HTTP body for PATCH /admin/employees/{userID}.
 9: // Every field is a pointer: if the JSON key is absent, the pointer is nil,
10: // and the repository will NOT update that column.
11: type PatchEmployeeProfileRequest struct {
12: 	FullName            *string         `json:"full_name"`
13: 	CorporateEmail      *string         `json:"corporate_email"`
14: 	Phone               *string         `json:"phone"`
15: 	Position            *string         `json:"position"`
16: 	Department          *string         `json:"department"`
17: 	BirthDate           *time.Time      `json:"birth_date"`
18: 	AvatarURL           *string         `json:"avatar_url"`
19: 	HireDate            *time.Time      `json:"hire_date"`
20: 	DismissalDate       *time.Time      `json:"dismissal_date"`
21: 	MedicalBookScanURL  *string         `json:"medical_book_scan_url"`
22: 	SpecialZoneAccess   *bool           `json:"special_zone_access"`
23: 	GDPTrainingHistory  json.RawMessage `json:"gdp_training_history"`
24: }
25: 
26: // EmployeeProfileResponse is what the API returns (read-only, no pointers for non-nullable fields).
27: type EmployeeProfileResponse struct {
28: 	ID                 uint            `json:"id"`
29: 	UserID             uint            `json:"user_id"`
30: 	EmployeeCode       string          `json:"employee_code"`
31: 	FullName           string          `json:"full_name"`
32: 	CorporateEmail     string          `json:"corporate_email"`
33: 	Phone              string          `json:"phone"`
34: 	Position           string          `json:"position"`
35: 	Department         string          `json:"department"`
36: 	BirthDate          time.Time       `json:"birth_date"`
37: 	AvatarURL          string          `json:"avatar_url"`
38: 	HireDate           time.Time       `json:"hire_date"`
39: 	DismissalDate      *time.Time      `json:"dismissal_date,omitempty"`
40: 	MedicalBookScanURL string          `json:"medical_book_scan_url"`
41: 	SpecialZoneAccess  bool            `json:"special_zone_access"`
42: 	GDPTrainingHistory json.RawMessage `json:"gdp_training_history"`
43: }
````

## File: backend/internal/pkg/logger/logger.go
````go
 1: package logger
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"context"
 5: 	"log/slog"
 6: 	"os"
 7: )
 8: 
 9: // contextKey is unexported so nobody outside this package can clash with it.
10: type contextKey string
11: 
12: const (
13: 	keyRequestID contextKey = "request_id"
14: 	keyUserID    contextKey = "user_id"
15: 	keyIPAddress contextKey = "ip_address"
16: )
17: 
18: // Setup initialises the global slog logger.
19: // Call this ONCE in main() before http.ListenAndServe.
20: func Setup(env string) {
21: 	level := slog.LevelInfo
22: 	if env == "dev" {
23: 		level = slog.LevelDebug
24: 	}
25: 
26: 	handler := slog.NewJSONHandler(os.Stdout, &slog.HandlerOptions{
27: 		Level:     level,
28: 		AddSource: true, // adds "source":{"file":"...","line":N} to every record
29: 	})
30: 
31: 	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(handler))
32: }
33: 
34: // WithRequestID stores a request ID in the context.
35: func WithRequestID(ctx context.Context, requestID string) context.Context {
36: 	return context.WithValue(ctx, keyRequestID, requestID)
37: }
38: 
39: // WithUserID stores a user ID in the context.
40: func WithUserID(ctx context.Context, userID int) context.Context {
41: 	return context.WithValue(ctx, keyUserID, userID)
42: }
43: 
44: // WithIPAddress stores the request IP address in the context.
45: func WithIPAddress(ctx context.Context, ip string) context.Context {
46: 	return context.WithValue(ctx, keyIPAddress, ip)
47: }
48: 
49: // IPAddressFromContext returns the IP address stored in ctx, or empty string.
50: func IPAddressFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
51: 	ip, _ := ctx.Value(keyIPAddress).(string)
52: 	return ip
53: }
54: 
55: // UserIDFromContext returns the user ID stored in ctx by WithUserID, or 0.
56: func UserIDFromContext(ctx context.Context) int {
57: 	uid, _ := ctx.Value(keyUserID).(int)
58: 	return uid
59: }
60: 
61: // FromContext returns a *slog.Logger pre-enriched with
62: // request_id and user_id extracted from ctx.
63: // Always returns a usable logger even if context values are absent.
64: func FromContext(ctx context.Context) *slog.Logger {
65: 	attrs := []any{}
66: 
67: 	if rid, ok := ctx.Value(keyRequestID).(string); ok && rid != "" {
68: 		attrs = append(attrs, slog.String("request_id", rid))
69: 	}
70: 	if uid, ok := ctx.Value(keyUserID).(int); ok && uid != 0 {
71: 		attrs = append(attrs, slog.Int("user_id", uid))
72: 	}
73: 
74: 	return slog.Default().With(attrs...)
75: }
````

## File: backend/internal/pkg/mailer/mailer.go
````go
 1: package mailer
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"context"
 5: 	"crypto/tls"
 6: 	"log/slog"
 7: 	"net/smtp"
 8: 
 9: 	"github.com/jordan-wright/email"
10: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/domain" // Импорт твоих ошибок
11: )
12: 
13: type Mailer interface {
14: 	SendOTP(ctx context.Context, toEmail, code string) error
15: }
16: 
17: type Config struct {
18: 	SMTPServer string
19: 	SMTPPort   string
20: 	Username   string
21: 	Password   string
22: }
23: 
24: type smtpMailer struct {
25: 	cfg Config
26: }
27: 
28: func New(cfg Config) Mailer {
29: 	return &smtpMailer{cfg: cfg}
30: }
31: 
32: func (m *smtpMailer) SendOTP(ctx context.Context, toEmail, code string) error {
33: 	// 1. Сборка письма через библиотеку
34: 	e := email.NewEmail()
35: 	e.From = "Diplom Admin <" + m.cfg.Username + ">" // Простая конкатенация вместо fmt
36: 	e.To = []string{toEmail}
37: 	e.Subject = "Verification Code"
38: 	e.HTML = []byte("<h2>Your code: " + code + "</h2>") // Конкатенация для скорости
39: 
40: 	auth := smtp.PlainAuth("", m.cfg.Username, m.cfg.Password, m.cfg.SMTPServer)
41: 	addr := m.cfg.SMTPServer + ":" + m.cfg.SMTPPort
42: 
43: 	// 2. Отправка
44: 	err := e.SendWithTLS(
45: 		addr,
46: 		auth,
47: 		&tls.Config{ServerName: m.cfg.SMTPServer},
48: 	)
49: 
50: 	if err != nil {
51: 		// Создаем структурированную ошибку приложения
52: 		appErr := domain.NewAppError(
53: 			"mailer_send_error",
54: 			"failed to physically send email via SMTP",
55: 			err, // Оборачиваем реальную ошибку библиотеки
56: 			slog.String("target_email", toEmail),
57: 			slog.String("smtp_server", m.cfg.SMTPServer),
58: 		)
59: 		
60: 		// Логируем через встроенный метод AppError
61: 		appErr.LogError(ctx)
62: 		return appErr
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	return nil
66: }
````

## File: backend/internal/repository/dao/audit_log_dao.go
````go
 1: package dao
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"encoding/json"
 5: 	"time"
 6: )
 7: 
 8: // AuditLogDAO — GORM snapshot of the audit_logs table.
 9: // gorm tags live here; the domain model (domain.AuditLog) has no ORM dependency.
10: type AuditLogDAO struct {
11: 	ID        string          `gorm:"primaryKey;type:uuid;default:gen_random_uuid()"`
12: 	UserID    *int            `gorm:"column:user_id"`
13: 	Action    string          `gorm:"column:action;type:varchar(255);not null"`
14: 	Entity    string          `gorm:"column:entity;type:varchar(100);not null"`
15: 	EntityID  string          `gorm:"column:entity_id;type:varchar(100)"`
16: 	OldValues json.RawMessage `gorm:"column:old_values;type:jsonb"`
17: 	NewValues json.RawMessage `gorm:"column:new_values;type:jsonb"`
18: 	IPAddress *string         `gorm:"column:ip_address;type:inet"`
19: 	// Immutability chain fields (Phase 3, currently NULL / mock):
20: 	PrevHash string `gorm:"column:prev_hash;type:text"`
21: 	LogHash  string `gorm:"column:log_hash;type:text"`
22: 	CreatedAt time.Time `gorm:"column:created_at;autoCreateTime"`
23: }
24: 
25: // TableName explicitly maps this DAO to the audit_logs table.
26: func (AuditLogDAO) TableName() string { return "audit_logs" }
````

## File: backend/internal/repository/dao/employee_profile_dao.go
````go
 1: package dao
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"encoding/json"
 5: 	"time"
 6: )
 7: 
 8: // EmployeeProfileDAO — GORM snapshot of the employee_profiles table.
 9: type EmployeeProfileDAO struct {
10: 	ID                 uint       `gorm:"primaryKey;autoIncrement"`
11: 	UserID             uint       `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null"`
12: 	EmployeeCode       string     `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null;type:varchar(100)"`
13: 	FullName           string     `gorm:"not null;type:varchar(255)"`
14: 	CorporateEmail     string     `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null;type:varchar(255)"`
15: 	Phone              string     `gorm:"uniqueIndex;not null;type:varchar(20)"`
16: 	Position           string     `gorm:"not null;type:varchar(255)"`
17: 	Department         string     `gorm:"not null;type:varchar(255)"`
18: 	BirthDate          time.Time  `gorm:"not null;type:date"`
19: 	AvatarURL          string     `gorm:"type:text"`
20: 	HireDate           time.Time  `gorm:"not null;type:date"`
21: 	DismissalDate      *time.Time `gorm:"type:date"`
22: 	// New ERP fields:
23: 	MedicalBookScanURL string          `gorm:"column:medical_book_scan_url;type:text"`
24: 	SpecialZoneAccess  bool            `gorm:"column:special_zone_access;default:false;not null"`
25: 	GDPTrainingHistory json.RawMessage `gorm:"column:gdp_training_history;type:jsonb;default:'[]';not null"`
26: }
27: 
28: func (EmployeeProfileDAO) TableName() string { return "employee_profiles" }
````

## File: backend/internal/repository/hooks/audit_hook.go
````go
  1: // Package hooks provides a GORM plugin that automatically writes an audit-log
  2: // row to the audit_logs table for every CREATE, UPDATE and DELETE operation.
  3: //
  4: // Usage (register once after gorm.Open):
  5: //
  6: //	if err := db.Use(&hooks.AuditPlugin{}); err != nil { ... }
  7: package hooks
  8: 
  9: import (
 10: 	"context"
 11: 	"encoding/json"
 12: 	"fmt"
 13: 	"reflect"
 14: 	"strings"
 15: 
 16: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/pkg/logger"
 17: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/repository/dao"
 18: 	"gorm.io/gorm"
 19: )
 20: 
 21: // skipTables contains table names that must never be audited.
 22: var skipTables = map[string]bool{
 23: 	"audit_logs":       true, // prevent infinite recursion
 24: 	"refresh_tokens":   true, // high-frequency token churn, no business audit value
 25: 	"environment_logs": true, // sensor data stream
 26: }
 27: 
 28: // settingOldValues is the Statement.Settings key used to pass the pre-update
 29: // JSON snapshot from beforeUpdate to afterUpdate.
 30: const settingOldValues = "audit:old_values"
 31: 
 32: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 33: // AuditPlugin
 34: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 35: 
 36: // AuditPlugin is a GORM plugin. Register it once with db.Use(&AuditPlugin{}).
 37: type AuditPlugin struct{}
 38: 
 39: // Name implements the gorm.Plugin interface.
 40: func (p *AuditPlugin) Name() string { return "audit_plugin" }
 41: 
 42: // Initialize registers all audit callbacks on db.
 43: func (p *AuditPlugin) Initialize(db *gorm.DB) error {
 44: 	cbs := []struct {
 45: 		scope  string
 46: 		pos    string // "before:<name>" or "after:<name>"
 47: 		name   string
 48: 		fn     func(*gorm.DB)
 49: 	}{
 50: 		{"update", "before", "audit:before_update", beforeUpdate},
 51: 		{"create", "after", "audit:after_create", afterCreate},
 52: 		{"update", "after", "audit:after_update", afterUpdate},
 53: 		{"delete", "after", "audit:after_delete", afterDelete},
 54: 	}
 55: 
 56: 	for _, cb := range cbs {
 57: 		var err error
 58: 		switch cb.scope {
 59: 		case "create":
 60: 			switch cb.pos {
 61: 			case "after":
 62: 				err = db.Callback().Create().After("gorm:create").Register(cb.name, cb.fn)
 63: 			}
 64: 		case "update":
 65: 			switch cb.pos {
 66: 			case "before":
 67: 				err = db.Callback().Update().Before("gorm:update").Register(cb.name, cb.fn)
 68: 			case "after":
 69: 				err = db.Callback().Update().After("gorm:update").Register(cb.name, cb.fn)
 70: 			}
 71: 		case "delete":
 72: 			switch cb.pos {
 73: 			case "after":
 74: 				err = db.Callback().Delete().After("gorm:delete").Register(cb.name, cb.fn)
 75: 			}
 76: 		}
 77: 		if err != nil {
 78: 			return fmt.Errorf("audit_plugin: register %s: %w", cb.name, err)
 79: 		}
 80: 	}
 81: 	return nil
 82: }
 83: 
 84: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 85: // Callbacks
 86: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 87: 
 88: // beforeUpdate captures the current DB row as old_values and stores it in
 89: // Statement.Settings so afterUpdate can persist it alongside new_values.
 90: func beforeUpdate(db *gorm.DB) {
 91: 	if db.Statement == nil || shouldSkip(db) || db.Statement.Model == nil {
 92: 		return
 93: 	}
 94: 
 95: 	cond := primaryKeyCondition(db)
 96: 	if cond == "" {
 97: 		return
 98: 	}
 99: 
100: 	var snapshot map[string]interface{}
101: 	if err := db.Session(&gorm.Session{NewDB: true, SkipHooks: true}).
102: 		Table(db.Statement.Table).
103: 		Where(cond).
104: 		Take(&snapshot).Error; err != nil {
105: 		return // non-fatal: old_values will be NULL in the audit log
106: 	}
107: 
108: 	if b, err := json.Marshal(snapshot); err == nil {
109: 		db.Statement.Settings.Store(settingOldValues, b)
110: 	}
111: }
112: 
113: // afterCreate writes a "create" audit entry.
114: func afterCreate(db *gorm.DB) {
115: 	if db.Statement == nil || shouldSkip(db) {
116: 		return
117: 	}
118: 	insertAuditLog(db, "create", nil, marshalModel(db))
119: }
120: 
121: // afterUpdate writes an "update" audit entry with before/after snapshots.
122: func afterUpdate(db *gorm.DB) {
123: 	if db.Statement == nil || shouldSkip(db) {
124: 		return
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	var oldValues json.RawMessage
128: 	if v, ok := db.Statement.Settings.Load(settingOldValues); ok {
129: 		if b, ok := v.([]byte); ok {
130: 			oldValues = b
131: 		}
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	insertAuditLog(db, "update", oldValues, marshalModel(db))
135: }
136: 
137: // afterDelete writes a "delete" audit entry (old = deleted record, new = nil).
138: func afterDelete(db *gorm.DB) {
139: 	if db.Statement == nil || shouldSkip(db) {
140: 		return
141: 	}
142: 	insertAuditLog(db, "delete", marshalModel(db), nil)
143: }
144: 
145: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146: // Internal helpers
147: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148: 
149: // shouldSkip returns true when the statement targets a skipped table or has
150: // already encountered an error (we do not audit failed operations).
151: func shouldSkip(db *gorm.DB) bool {
152: 	if db.Error != nil {
153: 		return true
154: 	}
155: 	return skipTables[db.Statement.Table]
156: }
157: 
158: // insertAuditLog builds and persists one audit_logs row.
159: func insertAuditLog(db *gorm.DB, action string, oldValues, newValues json.RawMessage) {
160: 	ctx := stmtContext(db)
161: 
162: 	entry := dao.AuditLogDAO{
163: 		Action:    action,
164: 		Entity:    db.Statement.Table,
165: 		EntityID:  extractPKValue(db),
166: 		OldValues: oldValues,
167: 		NewValues: newValues,
168: 	}
169: 
170: 	if ip := logger.IPAddressFromContext(ctx); ip != "" {
171: 		entry.IPAddress = &ip
172: 	}
173: 
174: 	if uid := logger.UserIDFromContext(ctx); uid != 0 {
175: 		entry.UserID = &uid
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	// Use a fresh session with a background context so this insert is
179: 	// independent of the parent transaction and never recurses into audit hooks.
180: 	db.Session(&gorm.Session{
181: 		NewDB:     true,
182: 		SkipHooks: true,
183: 		Context:   context.Background(),
184: 	}).Create(&entry)
185: }
186: 
187: // stmtContext returns the context bound to the current statement, or Background.
188: func stmtContext(db *gorm.DB) context.Context {
189: 	if db.Statement != nil && db.Statement.Context != nil {
190: 		return db.Statement.Context
191: 	}
192: 	return context.Background()
193: }
194: 
195: // primaryKeyCondition builds a "col = 'val'" WHERE clause for the primary key
196: // of the statement model. Returns empty string when the schema is unavailable.
197: func primaryKeyCondition(db *gorm.DB) string {
198: 	if db.Statement.Schema == nil || db.Statement.Model == nil {
199: 		return ""
200: 	}
201: 	
202: 	modelVal := reflect.Indirect(reflect.ValueOf(db.Statement.Model))
203: 	if modelVal.Kind() == reflect.Slice {
204: 		if modelVal.Len() == 0 {
205: 			return ""
206: 		}
207: 		modelVal = reflect.Indirect(modelVal.Index(0))
208: 	}
209: 	if modelVal.Kind() != reflect.Struct {
210: 		return ""
211: 	}
212: 	
213: 	// Create a pointer to the struct so field.ValueOf works correctly
214: 	ptr := reflect.New(modelVal.Type())
215: 	ptr.Elem().Set(modelVal)
216: 
217: 	var parts []string
218: 	for _, f := range db.Statement.Schema.PrimaryFields {
219: 		val, zero := f.ValueOf(db.Statement.Context, ptr)
220: 		if zero {
221: 			continue
222: 		}
223: 		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s = '%v'", f.DBName, val))
224: 	}
225: 	return strings.Join(parts, " AND ")
226: }
227: 
228: // extractPKValue returns the primary key value as a string (for entity_id).
229: func extractPKValue(db *gorm.DB) string {
230: 	if db.Statement.Schema == nil || db.Statement.Model == nil {
231: 		return ""
232: 	}
233: 	
234: 	modelVal := reflect.Indirect(reflect.ValueOf(db.Statement.Model))
235: 	if modelVal.Kind() == reflect.Slice {
236: 		if modelVal.Len() == 0 {
237: 			return ""
238: 		}
239: 		modelVal = reflect.Indirect(modelVal.Index(0))
240: 	}
241: 	if modelVal.Kind() != reflect.Struct {
242: 		return ""
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	ptr := reflect.New(modelVal.Type())
246: 	ptr.Elem().Set(modelVal)
247: 
248: 	for _, f := range db.Statement.Schema.PrimaryFields {
249: 		val, _ := f.ValueOf(db.Statement.Context, ptr)
250: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%v", val)
251: 	}
252: 	return ""
253: }
254: 
255: // marshalModel serialises db.Statement.Dest (preferred) or .Model to JSON.
256: func marshalModel(db *gorm.DB) json.RawMessage {
257: 	target := db.Statement.Dest
258: 	if target == nil {
259: 		target = db.Statement.Model
260: 	}
261: 	if target == nil {
262: 		return nil
263: 	}
264: 	b, _ := json.Marshal(target)
265: 	return b
266: }
````

## File: backend/internal/service/batch_service.go
````go
 1: package service
 2: 
 3: import (
 4: 	"context"
 5: 
 6: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/domain"
 7: )
 8: 
 9: type batchService struct {
10: 	repo domain.BatchRepository
11: }
12: 
13: func NewBatchService(repo domain.BatchRepository) domain.BatchService {
14: 	return &batchService{repo: repo}
15: }
16: 
17: func (s *batchService) ListBatches(ctx context.Context, filter domain.BatchFilter) ([]domain.Batch, int, error) {
18: 	return s.repo.List(ctx, filter)
19: }
20: 
21: func (s *batchService) GetBatch(ctx context.Context, id string) (*domain.Batch, error) {
22: 	batch, err := s.repo.GetByID(ctx, id)
23: 	if err != nil {
24: 		return nil, err
25: 	}
26: 	if batch == nil {
27: 		return nil, domain.ErrBatchNotFound
28: 	}
29: 	return batch, nil
30: }
31: 
32: func (s *batchService) UpdateStatus(ctx context.Context, callerRole domain.UserRole, id string, status domain.BatchStatus) error {
33: 	// Only Manager or Admin can manually change batch status
34: 	if callerRole != domain.RoleAdmin && callerRole != domain.RoleWarehouseManager {
35: 		return domain.ErrInsufficientPerms
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	batch, err := s.repo.GetByID(ctx, id)
39: 	if err != nil {
40: 		return err
41: 	}
42: 	if batch == nil {
43: 		return domain.ErrBatchNotFound
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	batch.Status = status
47: 	return s.repo.Update(ctx, batch)
48: }
49: 
50: func (s *batchService) TransferBatch(ctx context.Context, callerRole domain.UserRole, id string, targetZoneID string) error {
51: 	// Storekeeper, WarehouseManager and Admin can move goods; Pharmacist cannot
52: 	if callerRole == domain.RolePharmacist {
53: 		return domain.ErrInsufficientPerms
54: 	}
55: 	batch, err := s.repo.GetByID(ctx, id)
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return err
58: 	}
59: 	if batch == nil {
60: 		return domain.ErrBatchNotFound
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	batch.ZoneID = &targetZoneID
64: 	return s.repo.Update(ctx, batch)
65: }
````

## File: backend/internal/service/token_service_test.go
````go
  1: package service_test
  2: 
  3: import (
  4: 	"testing"
  5: 	"time"
  6: 
  7: 	"github.com/google/uuid"
  8: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/domain"
  9: 	"github.com/ima/diplom-backend/internal/service"
 10: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
 11: 	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
 12: )
 13: 
 14: // newTestTokenService is a helper that creates a TokenService with known settings.
 15: func newTestTokenService() domain.TokenService {
 16: 	return service.NewTokenService("test-secret", 15*time.Minute, 15*24*time.Hour)
 17: }
 18: 
 19: func testUser(id int, role domain.UserRole) *domain.User {
 20: 	return &domain.User{
 21: 		ID:    id,
 22: 		Email: "test@example.com",
 23: 		Role:  role,
 24: 	}
 25: }
 26: 
 27: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 28: // GenerateAccessToken + ParseAccessToken
 29: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 30: 
 31: // Scenario: JWT round-trip
 32: //
 33: //	Given:  A user with id=42, role=admin, email=test@example.com and a sessionID
 34: //	When:   GenerateAccessToken is called, then ParseAccessToken is called on the result
 35: //	Then:   Claims match the original inputs exactly
 36: func TestTokenService_GenerateAndParseAccessToken_HappyPath(t *testing.T) {
 37: 	svc := newTestTokenService()
 38: 	user := testUser(42, domain.RoleAdmin)
 39: 	sessionID := uuid.New()
 40: 
 41: 	raw, err := svc.GenerateAccessToken(user, sessionID)
 42: 	require.NoError(t, err)
 43: 	assert.NotEmpty(t, raw)
 44: 
 45: 	claims, err := svc.ParseAccessToken(raw)
 46: 	require.NoError(t, err)
 47: 	require.NotNil(t, claims)
 48: 
 49: 	assert.Equal(t, 42, claims.UserID)
 50: 	assert.Equal(t, domain.RoleAdmin, claims.Role)
 51: 	assert.Equal(t, "test@example.com", claims.Email)
 52: 	assert.Equal(t, sessionID, claims.SessionID)
 53: }
 54: 
 55: // Scenario: Parse token signed with wrong secret
 56: //
 57: //	Given:  A token generated with secret "test-secret"
 58: //	When:   ParseAccessToken is called by a service using "wrong-secret"
 59: //	Then:   Error is returned, claims are nil
 60: func TestTokenService_ParseAccessToken_WrongSecret(t *testing.T) {
 61: 	svcA := newTestTokenService()
 62: 	svcB := service.NewTokenService("wrong-secret", 15*time.Minute, 15*24*time.Hour)
 63: 
 64: 	user := testUser(1, domain.RolePharmacist)
 65: 	raw, err := svcA.GenerateAccessToken(user, uuid.New())
 66: 	require.NoError(t, err)
 67: 
 68: 	claims, err := svcB.ParseAccessToken(raw)
 69: 	assert.Error(t, err)
 70: 	assert.Nil(t, claims)
 71: }
 72: 
 73: // Scenario: Parse expired token
 74: //
 75: //	Given:  A token with TTL = -1 second (already expired at generation time)
 76: //	When:   ParseAccessToken is called
 77: //	Then:   Error is returned
 78: func TestTokenService_ParseAccessToken_Expired(t *testing.T) {
 79: 	// TTL of -1 second → token is expired the moment it's created
 80: 	svc := service.NewTokenService("test-secret", -1*time.Second, 15*24*time.Hour)
 81: 	raw, err := svc.GenerateAccessToken(testUser(1, domain.RolePharmacist), uuid.New())
 82: 	require.NoError(t, err)
 83: 
 84: 	claims, err := svc.ParseAccessToken(raw)
 85: 	assert.Error(t, err)
 86: 	assert.Nil(t, claims)
 87: }
 88: 
 89: // Scenario: Tampered token payload
 90: //
 91: //	Given:  A valid JWT whose payload has been modified after signing
 92: //	When:   ParseAccessToken is called
 93: //	Then:   Signature verification fails → error
 94: func TestTokenService_ParseAccessToken_Tampered(t *testing.T) {
 95: 	svc := newTestTokenService()
 96: 	raw, err := svc.GenerateAccessToken(testUser(1, domain.RolePharmacist), uuid.New())
 97: 	require.NoError(t, err)
 98: 
 99: 	// Flip the last character of the signature segment reliably
100: 	lastChar := "X"
101: 	if raw[len(raw)-1] == 'X' {
102: 		lastChar = "Y"
103: 	}
104: 	tampered := raw[:len(raw)-1] + lastChar
105: 	claims, err := svc.ParseAccessToken(tampered)
106: 	assert.Error(t, err)
107: 	assert.Nil(t, claims)
108: }
109: 
110: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111: // HashToken
112: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113: 
114: // Scenario: SHA-256 hash is deterministic and collision-resistant
115: //
116: //	Given:  Two distinct inputs "abc123" and "abc124"
117: //	Then:   Same input → same output (deterministic)
118: //	And:    Different inputs → different outputs
119: func TestTokenService_HashToken(t *testing.T) {
120: 	svc := newTestTokenService()
121: 
122: 	h1 := svc.HashToken("abc123")
123: 	h2 := svc.HashToken("abc123")
124: 	h3 := svc.HashToken("abc124")
125: 
126: 	assert.Equal(t, h1, h2, "hash must be deterministic")
127: 	assert.NotEqual(t, h1, h3, "different inputs must produce different hashes")
128: 	assert.Len(t, h1, 64, "SHA-256 hex digest must be 64 characters")
129: }
130: 
131: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132: // GenerateRefreshToken
133: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134: 
135: // Scenario: Refresh tokens are unique and hash is consistent
136: //
137: //	Given:  Two calls to GenerateRefreshToken
138: //	Then:   Raw values are different (collision-free)
139: //	And:    HashToken(raw) == hash returned by GenerateRefreshToken
140: func TestTokenService_GenerateRefreshToken(t *testing.T) {
141: 	svc := newTestTokenService()
142: 
143: 	raw1, hash1, err := svc.GenerateRefreshToken()
144: 	require.NoError(t, err)
145: 
146: 	raw2, hash2, err := svc.GenerateRefreshToken()
147: 	require.NoError(t, err)
148: 
149: 	assert.NotEmpty(t, raw1)
150: 	assert.NotEmpty(t, raw2)
151: 	assert.NotEqual(t, raw1, raw2, "tokens must be unique across calls")
152: 	assert.NotEqual(t, hash1, hash2, "hashes must differ for different tokens")
153: 
154: 	// Hash consistency: manually hashing raw must match the returned hash
155: 	assert.Equal(t, svc.HashToken(raw1), hash1, "manual hash must equal returned hash")
156: 	assert.Equal(t, svc.HashToken(raw2), hash2, "manual hash must equal returned hash")
157: }
````

## File: backend/migrate/000001_init_enums.up.sql
````sql
 1: -- ============================================================
 2: -- 000001_init_enums.up.sql
 3: -- All application-level ENUM types in a single migration.
 4: -- Replaces the original partial 000001 + scattered ALTER TYPE calls.
 5: -- ============================================================
 6: 
 7: -- Roles
 8: CREATE TYPE user_role AS ENUM (
 9:     'admin',
10:     'qp',               -- Уполномоченное лицо / QP
11:     'warehouse_manager',
12:     'storekeeper',
13:     'pharmacist'
14: );
15: 
16: -- Batch (series) status
17: CREATE TYPE batch_status AS ENUM (
18:     'quarantine',  -- accepted, awaiting inspection
19:     'available',   -- released for distribution
20:     'rejected',    -- rejected at acceptance
21:     'blocked'      -- blocked (STOP signal / Roszdravnadzor)
22: );
23: 
24: -- Warehouse zone type
25: CREATE TYPE zone_type AS ENUM (
26:     'ambient',
27:     'cold_chain',
28:     'flammable',
29:     'safe_strong'   -- safe/narcotic (НС/ПВ)
30: );
31: 
32: -- Shift for environment journal
33: CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('morning', 'evening');
34: 
35: -- Claim type
36: CREATE TYPE claim_type AS ENUM (
37:     'recall',               -- Roszdravnadzor withdrawal
38:     'return_from_pharmacy', -- return from pharmacy
39:     'return_to_supplier',   -- return to supplier
40:     'defect'                -- manufacturing defect
41: );
42: 
43: -- Purchase type
44: CREATE TYPE purchase_type AS ENUM ('direct', 'tender', 'state');
45: 
46: -- Order type
47: CREATE TYPE order_type AS ENUM ('regular', 'cito');
48: 
49: -- Order status
50: CREATE TYPE order_status AS ENUM ('new', 'assembling', 'assembled', 'shipped', 'cancelled');
51: 
52: -- Inventory status
53: CREATE TYPE inventory_status AS ENUM ('draft', 'active', 'completed', 'cancelled');
54: 
55: -- GDP training result
56: CREATE TYPE training_result AS ENUM ('pass', 'fail');
````

## File: backend/migrate/000004_create_warehouse_zones.up.sql
````sql
 1: -- ============================================================
 2: -- 000004_create_warehouse_zones.up.sql
 3: -- Warehouse zones.
 4: -- ============================================================
 5: 
 6: CREATE TABLE warehouse_zones (
 7:     id           UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
 8:     name         VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
 9:     type         zone_type NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ambient',
10:     description  TEXT,
11:     temp_min     NUMERIC(5,2),
12:     temp_max     NUMERIC(5,2),
13:     capacity     INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
14:     is_active    BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
15:     created_at   TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
16:     updated_at   TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
17: );
````

````sql
36: );
````
