## File: backend/migrate/000016_create_environment_logs.up.sql
````sql
 1: -- ============================================================
 2: -- 000016_create_environment_logs.up.sql
 3: -- Environment monitoring journal — Phase 3.
 4: -- One record per shift per day per zone.
 5: -- ============================================================
 6: 
 7: CREATE TABLE environment_logs (
 8:     id                   UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
 9:     zone_id              UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES warehouse_zones(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
10:     digital_signature_id UUID,  -- employee digital signature (not implemented)
11:     recorded_by          INT NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
12:     shift                shift_type NOT NULL,
13:     temperature          NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL,
14:     humidity             NUMERIC(5,2) NOT NULL,
15:     notes                TEXT,
16:     recorded_at          TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
17: );
18: 
19: -- Вместо UNIQUE внутри CREATE TABLE используем UNIQUE INDEX
20: -- Это позволяет использовать приведение к DATE
21: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_env_logs_unique_shift
22:     ON environment_logs (zone_id, CAST(recorded_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AS DATE), shift);
23: 
24: CREATE INDEX idx_env_logs_zone_date ON environment_logs(zone_id, recorded_at);
````

## File: backend/Dockerfile
````
 1: FROM golang:1.25-alpine AS builder
 2: WORKDIR /app
 3: COPY go.mod go.sum ./
 4: RUN go mod download
 5: COPY . .
 6: RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -o server ./cmd/main.go
 7: 
 8: FROM alpine:latest
 9: WORKDIR /app
10: # Копируем ТОЛЬКО бинарник
11: COPY --from=builder /app/server .
12: 
13: # .env НЕ КОПИРУЕМ. Данные придут через Environment Variables
14: EXPOSE 8080
15: CMD ["./server"]
````

## File: docs/api/auth-send-code.md
````markdown
 1: # POST `/auth/send-code`
 2: 
 3: > Отправить 6-значный OTP-код на email сотрудника.
 4: 
 5: **Auth:** Нет  
 6: **Route file:** `internal/handler/auth_handler.go`
 7: 
 8: ---
 9: 
10: ## Хранилище
11: 
12: OTP-коды хранятся **исключительно в Valkey (Redis)** — БД не используется.  
13: TTL истечения управляется самим Valkey автоматически.
14: 
15: ### Таблица `users` (PostgreSQL, только чтение)
16: 
17: - Найти пользователя по `email` → `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?`
18: - **Нет** в таблице → вернуть `404 user_not_found`
19: 
20: ### Valkey-ключи
21: 
22: | Ключ | Значение | TTL | Назначение |
23: |------|----------|-----|------------|
24: | `otp:hash:{user_id}` | HMAC-SHA256 hex кода | 600 с | Хранит хеш для верификации |
25: | `otp:attempts:{user_id}` | INT (счётчик) | 600 с | Счётчик неверных попыток |
26: | `otp:cooldown:{user_id}` | `1` | 60 с | Rate-limit: не даёт запросить новый код чаще раза в минуту |
27: 
28: **Rate-limit:** перед созданием кода проверить наличие `otp:cooldown:{user_id}`.  
29: Если ключ существует → вернуть `429 too_many_requests`.
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## Функции в программе
34: 
35: ### Handler
36: 
37: ```go
38: // Имя (планируется): sendCode(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
39: // Файл: internal/handler/auth_handler.go
40: // Route: POST /auth/send-code
41: ```
42: 
43: **Шаги:**
44: 1. Десериализовать `{ "email": "..." }` из тела запроса
45: 2. Вызвать `service.Auth.SendOTPCode(ctx, email)` *(не реализовано)*
46: 3. Вернуть `200 { "message": "code_sent", "expires_in": 600 }`
47: 
48: ### Service
49: 
50: ```go
51: // interface: domain.AuthService
52: // method: SendOTPCode(ctx, email) error
53: ```
54: 
55: **Шаги:**
56: 1. `userRepo.FindByEmail(ctx, email)` → `ErrUserNotFound` → 404
57: 2. Rate-limit: `otpStore.HasCooldown(ctx, user.ID)` → если есть → `ErrOTPCooldown` → 429
58: 3. Сгенерировать 6 цифр: `crypto/rand → fmt.Sprintf("%06d", n%1000000)`
59: 4. Хеш: `hmacSHA256(code, cfg.OTPHMACSecret)` → `hex.EncodeToString`
60: 5. `otpStore.Set(ctx, user.ID, hash, 600s)` — сохранить хеш в Valkey
61: 6. `otpStore.SetCooldown(ctx, user.ID, 60s)` — выставить кулдаун
62: 7. Отправить email через UniSender
63: 
64: ### OTP Store (Valkey)
65: 
66: ```go
67: // Нужно реализовать: domain.OTPStore (интерфейс)
68: // Файл: internal/repository/cache/otp_store.go
69: // методы:
70: //   Set(ctx, userID, hash string, ttl time.Duration) error
71: //   Get(ctx, userID int) (hash string, attempts int, error)
72: //   IncrAttempts(ctx, userID int) (int, error)
73: //   Delete(ctx, userID int) error
74: //   HasCooldown(ctx, userID int) (bool, error)
75: //   SetCooldown(ctx, userID int, ttl time.Duration) error
76: ```
````

## File: docs/api/auth-verify-code.md
````markdown
 1: # POST `/auth/verify-code`
 2: 
 3: > Проверить OTP-код и получить пару токенов (access + refresh cookie).
 4: 
 5: **Auth:** Нет  
 6: **Route file:** `internal/handler/auth_handler.go`
 7: 
 8: ---
 9: 
10: ## Хранилище
11: 
12: OTP-коды хранятся **исключительно в Valkey (Redis)** — таблицы `otp_codes` в БД нет и не планируется.
13: 
14: ### Таблица `users` (PostgreSQL, только чтение)
15: 
16: - Найти пользователя по `email`
17: - Пользователь должен быть не заблокирован, иначе `403`
18: 
19: ### Valkey-ключи (чтение / мутация)
20: 
21: | Ключ | Операция |
22: |------|----------|
23: | `otp:hash:{user_id}` | GET — получить сохранённый хеш |
24: | `otp:attempts:{user_id}` | INCR — увеличить счётчик при неверном коде |
25: | `otp:hash:{user_id}` + `otp:attempts:{user_id}` | DEL — удалить при успехе |
26: 
27: Если ключ `otp:hash:{user_id}` не существует — значит код истёк или никогда не запрашивался → `404 otp_not_found`.
28: 
29: ### Таблица `refresh_tokens` (PostgreSQL, запись)
30: 
31: - После успешной верификации → создать новую запись сессии (см. `issueTokens` в `auth_service.go`)
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## Функции в программе
36: 
37: ### Handler
38: 
39: ```go
40: // Имя (планируется): verifyCode(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
41: // Файл: internal/handler/auth_handler.go
42: // Route: POST /auth/verify-code
43: ```
44: 
45: **Шаги:**
46: 1. Десериализовать `{ "email": "...", "code": "482910" }`
47: 2. Вызвать `service.Auth.VerifyOTPCode(ctx, email, code, meta)` *(не реализовано)*
48: 3. Установить `httpOnly` cookie с refresh token (аналогично существующему `login` handler'у)
49: 4. Вернуть `200 { "access_token": "...", "expires_in": N }`
50: 
51: ### Service
52: 
53: ```go
54: // interface: domain.AuthService
55: // method: VerifyOTPCode(ctx, email, code string, meta SessionMeta) (*TokenPair, error)
56: ```
57: 
58: **Шаги:**
59: 1. `userRepo.FindByEmail(ctx, email)` → `ErrUserNotFound` → 404
60: 2. Проверить `user.IsBlocked` → `ErrUserBlocked` → 403
61: 3. `otpStore.Get(ctx, user.ID)` → если нет/истёк → `ErrOTPNotFound` → 404
62: 4. Если `attempts >= 5` → `ErrOTPMaxAttempts` → 429
63: 5. Сравнить: `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare(hmacSHA256(code, secret), storedHash)`
64: 6. Если не совпало: `otpStore.IncrAttempts(ctx, user.ID)` → `ErrOTPInvalid` → 401
65: 7. Если совпало: `otpStore.Delete(ctx, user.ID)` → `issueTokens(ctx, user, meta)`
66: 
67: ### Используемые существующие функции
68: 
69: - `authService.issueTokens()` — `internal/service/auth_service.go:209`
70: - `tokenSvc.GenerateAccessToken()` — `internal/service/token_service.go`
71: - `tokenSvc.GenerateRefreshToken()` — `internal/service/token_service.go`
72: - `sessionRepo.Create()` — `internal/repository/session.go:55`
````

## File: docs/backend/architecture.md
````markdown
  1: # Backend — Architecture & Onion Layers
  2: 
  3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Setup & Local Run →](./README.md)
  4: 
  5: ---
  6: 
  7: ## Onion Architecture
  8: 
  9: The backend is split into 4 concentric layers. Dependencies flow **inward only**: handlers depend on services, services depend on repositories via domain interfaces, and the domain core depends on nothing.
 10: 
 11: ```
 12: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 13: │  Handler  (internal/handler)                                  │
 14: │   HTTP handlers, request/response DTOs, middleware            │
 15: │                                                               │
 16: │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
 17: │  │  Service  (internal/service)                           │   │
 18: │  │   Business logic, repository orchestration             │   │
 19: │  │                                                         │   │
 20: │  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │   │
 21: │  │  │  Repository  (internal/repository)               │  │   │
 22: │  │  │   GORM implementations, DAO objects, SQL queries │  │   │
 23: │  │  │                                                   │  │   │
 24: │  │  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │  │   │
 25: │  │  │  │  Domain  (internal/domain)                 │  │  │   │
 26: │  │  │  │   Pure models, interfaces, errors          │  │  │   │
 27: │  │  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │  │   │
 28: │  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │   │
 29: │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
 30: └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
 31: ```
 32: 
 33: ### Layer Reference
 34: 
 35: | Layer | Package | Tags | Responsibility |
 36: |-------|---------|------|----------------|
 37: | **Handler (HTTP)** | `internal/handler` | — | Parse request, validate, call service |
 38: | **DTO** | `internal/handler/dto` | `json`, `validate` | API request/response shapes |
 39: | **Domain** | `internal/domain` | *(none)* | Pure Go structs, business interfaces |
 40: | **Service** | `internal/service` | — | Business logic, orchestration |
 41: | **Repository** | `internal/repository` | — | Interface implementations |
 42: | **DAO** | `internal/repository/dao` | `gorm` | DB table mirrors for GORM |
 43: 
 44: > **Rule:** Domain models know nothing about JSON or GORM. All tags live exclusively in DTO or DAO layers.
 45: 
 46: ---
 47: 
 48: ## Dependency Injection
 49: 
 50: All layers communicate through **interfaces** defined in `internal/domain`:
 51: 
 52: - `domain.UserRepository` — user data access
 53: - `domain.SessionRepository` — refresh token / session management
 54: - `domain.AuthService` — authentication business logic
 55: - `domain.TokenService` — JWT creation and validation
 56: - `domain.EmployeeProfileService` / `EmployeeProfileRepository` — employee profiles
 57: 
 58: Concrete implementations live in `repository/` and `service/`. This allows full mocking in tests.
 59: 
 60: ---
 61: 
 62: ## Entry Point (`cmd/main.go`)
 63: 
 64: Application starts in strict order:
 65: 
 66: 1. **Logger** (`internal/pkg/logger`) — `slog.Logger` with request context enrichment
 67: 2. **Config** (`config.Load()`) — `cleanenv` reads `.env` → env vars → exits on missing required vars
 68: 3. **Graceful shutdown** — `signal.NotifyContext` catches `SIGINT` / `SIGTERM`
 69: 4. **Database** — `repository.NewPostgresDB()` → GORM + pgx
 70: 5. **Layers** — `Repository → Service → Handler` (manual dependency injection)
 71: 6. **HTTP Server** — `domain.Server` (wrapper over `net/http.Server`)
 72: 7. **Bootstrap** — `bootstrap.SeedAdmin()` creates the first admin user if not present
 73: 
 74: ---
 75: 
 76: ## System Architecture Diagram (Frontend ↔ Backend)
 77: 
 78: ```mermaid
 79: graph LR
 80:     subgraph "Frontend (Next.js 16)"
 81:         A[Client Components] -- Interaction --> B[React State / Zod]
 82:         C[Server Components] -- Initial Data --> D[Next.js Fetch Cache]
 83:     end
 84: 
 85:     subgraph "Communication"
 86:         API[REST API / JSON]
 87:         Auth[JWT + HttpOnly Cookie]
 88:     end
 89: 
 90:     subgraph "Backend (Go 1.23+)"
 91:         H[Chi Router / Middleware] --> S[Service Layer]
 92:         S --> R[Repository Layer — GORM/PostgreSQL]
 93:         S --> V[Valkey Cache — OTP / Rate Limit]
 94:     end
 95: 
 96:     A <--> API
 97:     C <--> API
 98:     B <--> Auth
 99: ```
100: 
101: **Key principles:**
102: 1. **Stateless API** — backend holds no in-memory session state; uses JWT + PostgreSQL/Valkey
103: 2. **Onion Architecture** — strict layer separation (see above)
104: 3. **App Router (Next.js)** — Server Components for initial data + SEO; Client Components for interactive forms
105: 4. **Shared DTO** — Go backend structs correspond to TypeScript interfaces on the frontend
````

## File: docs/backend/auth.md
````markdown
 1: # Backend — Authentication & Sessions
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Routing & Middleware →](./routing.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## Token Scheme
 8: 
 9: | Token | Storage | TTL | Purpose |
10: |-------|---------|-----|---------|
11: | Access Token | Client memory | 15 min | API authorization (Bearer header) |
12: | Refresh Token | `httpOnly` Cookie | 15 days | Access token renewal |
13: 
14: **Access Token** — JWT signed with `HS256` using `JWT_SECRET`. Payload contains:
15: - `user_id` — user ID
16: - `role` — user role (`UserRole`)
17: - `email` — email address
18: - `session_id` — refresh token session UUID (used for revocation)
19: 
20: **Refresh Token** — random string (`crypto/rand`). Only its **SHA-256 hash** is stored in the DB. The raw token is sent to the client via `httpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict` cookie scoped to `/auth`.
21: 
22: ---
23: 
24: ## User Roles
25: 
26: Roles are defined as a PostgreSQL ENUM `user_role`:
27: 
28: | Role | Description |
29: |------|-------------|
30: | `admin` | System administrator |
31: | `qp` | Qualified Person (QP) — authorizes batch release |
32: | `warehouse_manager` | Warehouse manager |
33: | `storekeeper` | Storekeeper |
34: | `pharmacist` | Pharmacist (default role on user creation) |
35: 
36: ---
37: 
38: ## Google OAuth Flow
39: 
40: 1. Client obtains a Google ID Token (via Google Sign-In SDK)
41: 2. `POST /auth/google` → backend validates the ID Token via `idtoken.Validate`
42: 3. If email exists in DB → issue tokens
43: 4. If email not found → create new user with role `pharmacist` → issue tokens
44: 
45: ---
46: 
47: ## Refresh Token Theft Detection
48: 
49: When a **revoked** refresh token is reused:
50: - `sessionRepo.RevokeAllForUser()` — **all** active sessions for the user are revoked
51: - Returns `401 Unauthorized`
52: 
53: ---
54: 
55: ## Token Rotation
56: 
57: On every `/auth/refresh`:
58: 1. Old session is marked `revoked_at = NOW()`
59: 2. New session is created with a new refresh token
60: 3. New access token is issued
61: 
62: ---
63: 
64: ## OTP Codes (Valkey)
65: 
66: OTP codes are stored **only in Valkey**, never in PostgreSQL.
67: - Key: `otp:user:<user_id>` (Hash), TTL 600 sec
68: - Details: [../../docs/valkey-cache.md](../valkey-cache.md)
````

## File: docs/backend/domain-repository-service.md
````markdown
  1: # Backend — Domain, Repository & Service
  2: 
  3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Routing & Middleware →](./routing.md)
  4: 
  5: ---
  6: 
  7: ## Domain Layer (`internal/domain/`)
  8: 
  9: Pure Go structs **without ORM or JSON tags** — business fields only.
 10: 
 11: ### Key Models
 12: 
 13: ```go
 14: // User — system user
 15: type User struct {
 16:     ID          int
 17:     Email       string    // primary identifier
 18:     GoogleID    *string   // nullable (OAuth)
 19:     TelegramID  *int64    // nullable (OAuth)
 20:     Role        UserRole
 21:     NsPvAccess  bool      // access to NS/PV (narcotic/psychotropic substances)
 22:     UkepBound   bool      // qualified e-signature bound
 23:     IsBlocked   bool
 24:     CreatedAt   time.Time
 25:     UpdatedAt   time.Time
 26: }
 27: 
 28: // User roles (mirror of PostgreSQL ENUM user_role)
 29: RoleAdmin            UserRole = "admin"
 30: RoleQP               UserRole = "qp"
 31: RoleWarehouseManager UserRole = "warehouse_manager"
 32: RoleStorekeeper      UserRole = "storekeeper"
 33: RolePharmacist       UserRole = "pharmacist"
 34: 
 35: // RefreshToken — active session
 36: type RefreshToken struct {
 37:     ID        uuid.UUID
 38:     UserID    int
 39:     TokenHash string       // SHA-256 of raw token
 40:     ExpiresAt time.Time
 41:     UserAgent string
 42:     IPAddress string
 43:     Metadata  map[string]any
 44:     CreatedAt time.Time
 45:     RevokedAt *time.Time   // nil = active
 46: }
 47: 
 48: // EmployeeProfile — staff profile
 49: type EmployeeProfile struct {
 50:     ID                 uint
 51:     UserID             uint
 52:     EmployeeCode       string
 53:     FullName           string
 54:     CorporateEmail     string
 55:     Phone              string
 56:     Position           string
 57:     Department         string
 58:     BirthDate          time.Time
 59:     AvatarURL          string
 60:     HireDate           time.Time
 61:     DismissalDate      *time.Time
 62:     MedicalBookScanURL string
 63:     SpecialZoneAccess  bool
 64:     GDPTrainingHistory []GDPTrainingRecord  // JSONB array
 65: }
 66: ```
 67: 
 68: ### Errors (`errors.go`)
 69: 
 70: Sentinel errors (used with `errors.Is`):
 71: 
 72: ```go
 73: ErrUserNotFound            = "user not found"
 74: ErrEmailTaken              = "email already taken"
 75: ErrInvalidCreds            = "invalid credentials"
 76: ErrUserBlocked             = "account is blocked"
 77: ErrTokenExpired            = "token expired"
 78: ErrInvalidToken            = "invalid token"
 79: ErrSessionNotFound         = "session not found or terminated"
 80: ErrInsufficientPerms       = "insufficient permissions for this operation"
 81: ErrEmployeeProfileNotFound = "employee profile not found"
 82: ```
 83: 
 84: `AppError` — structured error with a code, message, and `slog.Attr` for logging.
 85: 
 86: ---
 87: 
 88: ## Repository Layer (`internal/repository/`)
 89: 
 90: ### PostgreSQL Connection (`postgres.go`)
 91: 
 92: ```go
 93: // GORM + pgx driver
 94: db, err := gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{})
 95: ```
 96: 
 97: ### DAO Pattern
 98: 
 99: Domain models and GORM are separated by **DAO structs** in `repository/dao/` which carry GORM tags:
100: 
101: - `UserDAO` → table `users`
102: - `SessionDAO` → table `refresh_tokens`
103: - `EmployeeProfileDAO` → table `employee_profiles`
104: 
105: Repositories convert between `DAO ↔ domain` via `toDomain()` / `fromDomain()` methods.
106: 
107: ### `UserRepository` (`user.go`)
108: 
109: | Method | SQL / GORM |
110: |--------|-----------|
111: | `FindByID` | `SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?` |
112: | `FindByEmail` | `WHERE email = ?` |
113: | `FindByGoogleID` | `WHERE google_id = ?` |
114: | `FindByTelegramID` | `WHERE telegram_id = ?` |
115: | `IsEmailTaken` | `COUNT(*) WHERE email = ?` |
116: | `Create` | `INSERT INTO users` |
117: | `UpdateRole` | `UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?` |
118: | `LinkGoogle` | `UPDATE users SET google_id = ?` |
119: | `LinkTelegram` | `UPDATE users SET telegram_id = ?` |
120: | `SetNsPvAccess` | `UPDATE users SET ns_pv_access = ?` |
121: | `SetBlocked` | `UPDATE users SET is_blocked = ?` |
122: | `FindProfileByUserID` | JOIN users + employee_profiles |
123: 
124: ### `SessionRepository` (`session.go`)
125: 
126: | Method | Description |
127: |--------|-------------|
128: | `Create` | Store new session (refresh token hash) |
129: | `FindByID` | Find session by UUID |
130: | `FindByTokenHash` | Find session by SHA-256 token hash |
131: | `FindActiveByUserID` | All non-expired, non-revoked sessions |
132: | `Revoke` | `SET revoked_at = NOW() WHERE id = ?` |
133: | `RevokeAllForUser` | `SET revoked_at = NOW() WHERE user_id = ?` |
134: | `DeleteExpired` | `DELETE WHERE expires_at < NOW()` (cleanup job) |
135: 
136: ---
137: 
138: ## Service Layer (`internal/service/`)
139: 
140: ### `Service` (`service.go`)
141: 
142: Service aggregator. Instantiated once in `main.go`:
143: 
144: ```go
145: service.NewService(repos, jwtSecret, googleClientID)
146: // Access Token TTL  = 15 minutes
147: // Refresh Token TTL = 15 days
148: ```
149: 
150: ### `AuthService` (`auth_service.go`)
151: 
152: | Method | Description |
153: |--------|-------------|
154: | `LoginWithGoogle` | `idtoken.Validate` → find/create user → `issueTokens` |
155: | `LoginWithTelegram` | **Stub** (`panic("implement...")`), needs bot token hash verification |
156: | `RefreshTokens` | `HashToken` → `FindByTokenHash` → theft check → revoke old → `issueTokens` |
157: | `RevokeSession` | Verify ownership or admin → `sessionRepo.Revoke` |
158: | `AssignRole` | Admin only → `userRepo.UpdateRole` |
159: | `SetBlocked` | Admin only → `userRepo.SetBlocked` |
160: 
161: **Private `issueTokens`:**
162: 1. Verify user is not blocked
163: 2. Generate raw refresh token (`crypto/rand`) + its SHA-256 hash
164: 3. Create `RefreshToken` record in DB
165: 4. Generate JWT access token with `session_id = RefreshToken.ID`
166: 
167: ### `TokenService` (`token_service.go`)
168: 
169: - `GenerateAccessToken(user, sessionID)` → JWT HS256
170: - `ParseAccessToken(token)` → `TokenClaims{UserID, Role, Email, SessionID}`
171: - `GenerateRefreshToken()` → `(rawToken, hashToken, error)`
172: - `HashToken(raw)` → SHA-256 hex
173: 
174: ### `EmployeeProfileService` (`employee_profile_service.go`)
175: 
176: CRUD for employee profiles. Checks caller permissions (admin or the user themselves).
````

## File: docs/backend/logging.md
````markdown
 1: # Backend — Logging
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Architecture →](./architecture.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## Logging System
 8: 
 9: Uses stdlib `log/slog` with structured output via a custom context-aware wrapper.
10: 
11: > **Critical Rule:** Never use `fmt.Println`, `fmt.Printf`, or any `fmt` print functions for logs or debug output.
12: > Always use the project logger:
13: > ```go
14: > log := logger.FromContext(ctx)
15: > log.Info("...", "key", value)
16: > ```
17: > Name the logger variable `log`, not `logger`.
18: 
19: ### Components
20: 
21: - `internal/pkg/logger` — wrapper:
22:   - Format selection: `APP_ENV=production` → JSON output, otherwise text
23:   - Context enrichment: `logger.WithUserID(ctx, id)`
24: - `logger.FromContext(ctx)` — extracts logger from context (injected by `AuthRequired` middleware)
25: - Custom `loggingMiddleware` in `Handler` logs every request:
26:   - method, path, status, latency, request_id
27: 
28: ### Log Levels
29: 
30: | Event | Level |
31: |-------|-------|
32: | Missing `.env` file | `WARN` |
33: | Successful server start | `INFO` |
34: | Incoming HTTP request | `INFO` |
35: | Business logic errors (`AppError`) | `ERROR` |
36: | Critical errors (panic recovery) | `ERROR` |
37: 
38: ### Request Log Example
39: 
40: ```json
41: {
42:   "time": "2026-03-28T10:00:00Z",
43:   "level": "INFO",
44:   "msg": "request completed",
45:   "method": "PATCH",
46:   "path": "/api/v1/admin/users/5/role",
47:   "status": 200,
48:   "latency_ms": 12,
49:   "request_id": "abc123",
50:   "user_id": 1
51: }
52: ```
````

## File: docs/backend/migrations.md
````markdown
 1: # Backend — Database Migrations
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Local Setup →](./README.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## Applying Migrations
 8: 
 9: SQL files are numbered and applied via `golang-migrate`. Each migration has `*.up.sql` (apply) and `*.down.sql` (rollback) files.
10: 
11: ```bash
12: # Apply all (via Docker Compose — preferred)
13: docker compose run --rm migrate
14: 
15: # Apply via CLI
16: migrate -path ./backend/migrate -database "postgres://..." up
17: ```
18: 
19: ---
20: 
21: ## Migration List
22: 
23: | Migration | Table / Change | Phase |
24: |-----------|---------------|-------|
25: | 000001 | ENUMs: `user_role`, `batch_status`, `zone_type`, `shift_type`, `claim_type`, `purchase_type`, `order_type`, `order_status`, `inventory_status`, `training_result` | 1 |
26: | 000002 | `users` — email-based, no login/password, with ERP roles | 1 |
27: | 000003 | `categories` | 1 |
28: | 000004 | `products` (formerly `medicaments`) — full redesign | 1 |
29: | 000005 | `warehouse_zones` (formerly `warehouses`) — zone management | 1 |
30: | 000006 | `suppliers` — supplier registry | 1 |
31: | 000007 | `batches` (formerly `stock_items`) — batch/series tracking | 1 |
32: | 000008 | `audit_logs` — with hash chain fields (Phase 3, mock) | 3 |
33: | 000009 | `employee_profiles` — extended GDP, medical book, zone access | 1 |
34: | 000010 | `refresh_tokens` — unchanged | 1 |
35: | 000011 | `inbound_receipts` + `inbound_positions` — goods receiving | 1 |
36: | 000012 | `product_photos` — drug product photos | 1 |
37: | 000013 | `settings` — system settings (seeded: `mos_percent=60`) | 1 |
38: | 000014 | `orders` + `order_items` — orders (FEFO) | 2 |
39: | 000015 | `inventory_sessions` + `inventory_items` + `inventory_samples` | 2 |
40: | 000016 | `claims` + `claim_photos` + `recalled_batches` | 3 |
41: | 000017 | `environment_logs` — warehouse environment journal | 3 |
42: 
43: ---
44: 
45: ## Deprecated / Removed Tables
46: 
47: The following tables from old migrations were replaced or removed:
48: 
49: | Old Table | Replacement |
50: |-----------|------------|
51: | `medicaments` | `products` |
52: | `warehouses` | `warehouse_zones` |
53: | `stock_items` | `batches` |
54: | `stock_operations` | `batches` + `order_items` |
55: | `users.login` | Removed — primary identifier is now `email` |
56: | `users.password_hash` | Removed — OAuth only (Google / Telegram) |
57: | `users.status` | Removed — replaced by `is_blocked` boolean |
58: 
59: → Full SQL schema: [`docs/other/migrate.md`](../Diplom/migrate.md)
````

## File: docs/backend/routing.md
````markdown
 1: # Backend — HTTP Router & Middleware
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Domain / Service / Repository →](./domain-repository-service.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## HTTP Router (`chi`)
 8: 
 9: Package `go-chi/chi/v5` — lightweight idiomatic router for `net/http`.
10: 
11: ### Global Middleware (registered in `handler.go`)
12: 
13: ```go
14: r.Use(middleware.RequestID)           // Adds X-Request-Id header
15: r.Use(middleware.Recoverer)           // Panic recovery → 500
16: r.Use(middleware.Timeout(60s))        // 60-second request timeout
17: r.Use(middleware.Heartbeat("/ping"))  // Healthcheck: GET /ping
18: r.Use(h.loggingMiddleware)            // Custom slog request logger
19: ```
20: 
21: ### Route Structure
22: 
23: ```
24: POST /auth/google                — Google OAuth (ID-token → access_token)
25: POST /auth/refresh               — Token refresh via cookie
26: POST /auth/logout                — Logout (revoke session + clear cookie)
27: 
28: /api/v1/ (middleware: AuthRequired — Bearer JWT check)
29:   DELETE /sessions/{sessionID}   — Revoke own session
30: 
31:   /admin/ (middleware: RequireRole("admin"))
32:     PATCH  /users/{id}/role      — Assign role
33:     PATCH  /users/{id}/blocked   — Block / unblock user
34:     DELETE /sessions/{sessionID} — Revoke any session
35:     GET    /employees/           — List employee profiles
36:     GET    /employees/{userID}   — Get employee profile
37:     PATCH  /employees/{userID}   — Update employee profile
38: ```
39: 
40: ---
41: 
42: ## Middleware (`internal/handler/middleware/`)
43: 
44: ### `AuthRequired` (`auth_middleware.go`)
45: 
46: Extracts JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header, validates via `domain.TokenService`, and stores in context:
47: 
48: ```go
49: ctx.Value(middleware.CtxUserID) → int       // User ID
50: ctx.Value(middleware.CtxRole)   → UserRole  // User role
51: ctx.Value(middleware.CtxEmail)  → string    // Email
52: ```
53: 
54: ### `RequireRole` (`rbac_middleware.go`)
55: 
56: Checks role from context. Accepts one or more allowed `UserRole` values. Returns `403 Forbidden` on mismatch.
57: 
58: ---
59: 
60: ## Handler Layer (`internal/handler/`)
61: 
62: ### `Handler` (`handler.go`)
63: 
64: Holds references to `service.Service` and `domain.TokenService`. The `Router()` method assembles the chi router.
65: 
66: ### DTOs (`internal/handler/dto/`)
67: 
68: Request/response structs with JSON tags, isolated from domain:
69: 
70: - `auth_dto.go` — `GoogleAuthRequest`, `TokenResponse`
71: - `user_dto.go` — `AssignRoleRequest`, `SetBlockedRequest`, `UserResponse`
72: - `employee_profile_dto.go` — `EmployeeProfileDTO`
73: - `admin_employee_dto.go` — `PatchEmployeeProfileRequest`, `EmployeeProfileResponse`
74: 
75: ### Handler Files
76: 
77: | File | Handlers |
78: |------|----------|
79: | `auth_handler.go` | `refresh`, `logout`, `clearRefreshCookie` |
80: | `oauth_handler.go` | `googleLogin` |
81: | `admin_handler.go` | `adminAssignRole`, `adminSetBlocked`, `revokeSession`, `adminRevokeSession` |
82: | `admin_employee_handler.go` | `adminListEmployeeProfiles`, `adminGetEmployeeProfile`, `adminPatchEmployeeProfile` |
````

## File: docs/backend/test.md
````markdown
 1: # Backend — Additional Testing Notes
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Testing Strategy →](../Diplom/testing.md)
 4: 
 5: ---
 6: 
 7: ## Docker-Based Testing Requirement
 8: 
 9: All backend tests must also pass the Docker container build without errors.
10: 
11: The full test environment requires:
12: - A running `backend` Docker container
13: - A running `valkey` container
14: - A running `postgres` container
15: 
16: > **Preferred approach:** Fully restart (re-create) the backend container on each test run to ensure a clean state.
17: 
18: ```bash
19: # Rebuild and restart backend container before running tests
20: docker compose --profile backend-all up -d --build --force-recreate backend
21: go test ./...
22: ```
23: 
24: ---
25: 
26: ## TODO
27: 
28: - [ ] Define integration test suite structure
29: - [ ] Set up `testcontainers-go` for isolated DB in CI
30: - [ ] Add coverage reporting to CI pipeline (target: ≥ 70%)
````

## File: docs/Diplom/testing.md
````markdown
 1: # Testing Strategy & Security Checklist
 2: 
 3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [CI/CD Policy →](../other/workflow.md)
 4: 
 5: Данный документ описывает обязательные уровни тестирования, ключевые сценарии и инструменты для обеспечения надежности и безопасности системы.
 6: 
 7: ---
 8: 
 9: ## 1. Методология и Инструменты
10: 
11: Для проекта выбрана пирамида тестирования с упором на интеграционные тесты бизнес-логики.
12: 
13: | Уровень | Инструменты | Описание |
14: |---------|-------------|----------|
15: | **Unit (Backend)** | `go test`, `stretchr/testify` | Тестирование чистых функций, валидаторов и хэширования. |
16: | **Unit (Frontend)** | `Vitest`, `React Testing Library` | Тестирование UI-компонентов и Zod-схем. |
17: | **Integration** | `httptest`, `sqlmock`, `testcontainers` | Проверка API-эндпоинтов с участием реальной БД (или Docker). |
18: | **E2E (End-to-End)** | `Playwright` | Проверка полных пользовательских путей в браузере. |
19: | **Security** | `gosec`, `pnpm audit`, `OWASP ZAP` | Статический анализ кода и сканирование уязвимостей. |
20: 
21: ---
22: 
23: ## 2. Обязательные тестовые сценарии (Критические)
24: 
25: ### 2.1. Аутентификация и Сессии
26: *   **TC-01: Верификация OTP.** Ввод верного кода $\rightarrow$ получение JWT. Ввод неверного кода 3 раза $\rightarrow$ блокировка попыток через Valkey.
27: *   **TC-02: Защита от кражи Refresh Token.** Попытка использовать один и тот же RT дважды $\rightarrow$ система должна отозвать все активные сессии пользователя (угроза компрометации).
28: *   **TC-03: RBAC (Доступы).** Попытка `Storekeeper` вызвать эндпоинт `/admin/users` $\rightarrow$ возврат `403 Forbidden`.
29: 
30: ### 2.2. Учет серий и FEFO (Бизнес-логика)
31: *   **TC-04: Корректность FEFO.** Создать 3 серии с разными сроками годности. Оформить заказ. Проверить, что система зарезервировала серию с самой ранней датой истечения.
32: *   **TC-05: Выпуск из карантина.** Попытка отгрузить серию в статусе `quarantine` $\rightarrow$ ошибка. Попытка перевести в `available` пользователем без роли `qp` $\rightarrow$ ошибка.
33: *   **TC-06: Блокировка STOP-сигнала.** При добавлении серии в `recalled_batches`, все существующие остатки этой серии на складе должны автоматически получить статус `blocked`.
34: 
35: ### 2.3. Складские операции
36: *   **TC-07: Журнал среды.** Валидация: ввод температуры 35°C в зону с режимом 2-8°C $\rightarrow$ запись создается, но система генерирует критический алерт (Warning).
37: *   **TC-08: Слепая инвентаризация.** При открытой сессии инвентаризации фронтенд не должен получать `expected_qty` из API до момента нажатия кнопки «Завершить».
38: 
39: ---
40: 
41: ## 3. Проверка безопасности (Security Checklist)
42: 
43: Обязательные проверки перед деплоем:
44: 
45: 1.  **JWT Integrity:**
46:     *   Проверка подписи (HS256).
47:     *   Проверка `exp` (истечение) и `aud` (аудитория).
48: 2.  **Input Validation:**
49:     *   Защита от XSS во всех текстовых полях карточки товара (особенно `description`).
50:     *   Защита от SQL Injection в поисковых запросах через GORM parameterized queries.
51: 3.  **Cookies Security:**
52:     *   `refresh_token` должен иметь флаги: `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, `SameSite=Strict`.
53: 4.  **Rate Limiting:**
54:     *   Ограничение на количество запросов `/auth/send-code` с одного IP (защита от спама/подбора).
55: 5.  **Sensitive Data:**
56:     *   Отсутствие паролей и токенов в логах (`audit_logs` не должен содержать `token_hash`).
57: 
58: ---
59: 
60: ## 4. Регламент CI/CD тестирования
61: 
62: *   **Pre-commit:** Запуск `lint` (golangci-lint, eslint).
63: *   **Pull Request:** Запуск Unit и Integration тестов. Покрытие кода (coverage) не ниже 70%.
64: *   **Staging:** Запуск дымовых (Smoke) E2E тестов в Playwright перед релизом.
````

## File: docs/frontend/README.md
````markdown
  1: # Frontend — Overview & Development Standards
  2: 
  3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md) | [Full Conventions →](./conventions.md) | [Forms UI Specs →](./forms/) | [Forms Logic →](./forms-logic.md)
  4: 
  5: > ⛔ **Branch Rule:** All frontend code **must** be written in `develop-frontend` only.
  6: > Direct commits to `main` or `develop-backend` are strictly forbidden.
  7: > Merging into `main` is done exclusively via the [`/merge-all`](../other/git-workflow.md) workflow.
  8: 
  9: Next.js 16 App Router application for the Pharmaceutical ERP system. TypeScript-strict, server-components-first.
 10: 
 11: > **AI Context & Navigation:** This README is your starting point. It contains the most critical mandates. For specific technical details, jump to the relevant sub-document:
 12: > - Writing components, styles, fetching data or dates? Read [**`conventions.md`**](./conventions.md).
 13: > - Need form UI / layouts? Check [**`forms/`**](./forms/).
 14: > - Writing form logic, API calls, or schemas? Use [**`forms-logic.md`**](./forms-logic.md) and verify with [`docs/api/swagger.json`](../../docs/api/swagger.json).
 15: > - Writing hooks / business logic? Read [**`hooks.md`**](./hooks.md) for Colocation and TDD rules.
 16: 
 17: ---
 18: 
 19: ## Tech Stack
 20: 
 21: | Technology | Version | Role |
 22: |-----------|---------|------|
 23: | **Next.js** | 16 (App Router) | Framework |
 24: | **React** | 19 | UI runtime |
 25: | **TypeScript** | Strict mode | Language |
 26: | **Tailwind CSS** | v4 | Styling |
 27: | **shadcn/ui** | Latest | Component library |
 28: | **lucide-react** | Latest | Icons (only) |
 29: | **Zod** | v4 | Schema validation |
 30: | **nuqs** | Latest | URL state management |
 31: | **date-fns** | v4 | Date formatting & calculations |
 32: 
 33: ---
 34: 
 35: ## Local Development
 36: 
 37: ```bash
 38: cd frontend
 39: pnpm install
 40: pnpm dev    # http://localhost:3000
 41: ```
 42: 
 43: Or via Docker Compose from the monorepo root:
 44: 
 45: ```bash
 46: docker compose --profile all up -d
 47: ```
 48: 
 49: ---
 50: 
 51: ## global.css — Immutability Mandate
 52: 
 53: > ⛔ **`src/app/globals.css` is frozen. Do not modify it under any circumstances.**
 54: 
 55: Rules:
 56: 1. **Zero new variables** — do not add colors, shadows, or border radii
 57: 2. **Zero raw Tailwind utilities** — never use `bg-gray-800`, `text-red-500`, etc.
 58: 3. **Semantic variables only** — use only what is already defined: `bg-primary`, `text-foreground`, `rounded-xl`, etc.
 59: 4. **Modifying shadcn base components** — requires explicit approval before any change
 60: 
 61: ```
 62: ✅  bg-primary          ❌  bg-gray-800
 63: ✅  text-destructive    ❌  text-red-500
 64: ✅  border-border       ❌  border-gray-200
 65: ✅  rounded-xl          ❌  rounded-[14px]
 66: ```
 67: 
 68: → Full variable reference: [`conventions.md — Styling`](./conventions.md#styling)
 69: 
 70: ---
 71: 
 72: ## Project Structure
 73: 
 74: ```
 75: frontend/src/
 76: ├── app/
 77: │   ├── (app)/          # Main pages (Header + Footer layout)
 78: │   │   ├── layout.tsx
 79: │   │   ├── page.tsx    # Dashboard
 80: │   │   └── search/
 81: │   ├── (auth)/         # Login pages (minimal layout)
 82: │   │   ├── layout.tsx
 83: │   │   └── auth/       # /auth/login, /auth/verify
 84: │   ├── (admin)/        # Admin section
 85: │   │   └── admin/
 86: │   ├── globals.css     # ⛔ FROZEN — all theme tokens (do not touch)
 87: │   └── layout.tsx      # Root layout (font, NuqsAdapter)
 88: ├── components/
 89: │   ├── ui/             # shadcn/ui components (base — do not modify without approval)
 90: │   ├── header.tsx      # "Dumb" — no fetch, no Zod inside
 91: │   └── footer.tsx
 92: ├── hooks/              # ← All business logic lives here (see §Folder Structure)
 93: │   └── README.md
 94: └── lib/
 95:     └── utils.ts        # cn() helper (clsx + tailwind-merge)
 96: ```
 97: 
 98: ---
 99: 
100: ## Folder Structure — Logic Isolation
101: 
102: ### The Core Rule
103: 
104: > **Components are dumb. Hooks are smart.**
105: 
106: | Where | What goes here |
107: |-------|---------------|
108: | `components/*.tsx` | JSX only — markup, props, calls to hooks |
109: | `hooks/use-*.ts` | Zod schemas, `useForm`, `fetch`, derived state |
110: | `app/**/page.tsx` | Layout assembly — calls Server Components or hooks only |
111: 
112: ### ✅ Correct Pattern
113: 
114: ```tsx
115: // hooks/use-login-form.ts  ← logic here
116: export function useLoginForm() {
117:   const form = useForm<z.infer<typeof emailSchema>>({
118:     resolver: zodResolver(emailSchema),
119:   })
120:   const onSubmit = async (data: z.infer<typeof emailSchema>) => {
121:     const res = await fetch("/api/v1/auth/send-code", { ... })
122:   }
123:   return { form, onSubmit }
124: }
125: 
126: // components/login-form.tsx  ← UI only
127: export function LoginForm() {
128:   const { form, onSubmit } = useLoginForm()  // consumed here
129:   return <Form {...form}><Input .../><Button .../></Form>
130: }
131: ```
132: 
133: ### ❌ Forbidden Pattern
134: 
135: ```tsx
136: // ❌ Logic inside a page or component
137: export default function LoginPage() {
138:   const form = useForm(...)    // ← forbidden
139:   const onSubmit = async () => {
140:     await fetch(...)           // ← forbidden
141:   }
142: }
143: ```
144: 
145: ### Colocation Rule (Go-style)
146: 
147: Test file lives **in the same directory** as its source:
148: 
149: ```
150: hooks/
151: ├── use-auth-login.ts
152: ├── use-auth-login.test.ts    ← here, not in __tests__/
153: 
154: components/
155: ├── login-form.tsx
156: └── login-form.test.tsx       ← here, not in __tests__/
157: ```
158: 
159: ---
160: 
161: ## Stitch & UI Prototyping Workflow
162: 
163: ### Task Split
164: 
165: UI design and logic implementation are **two separate, independent tasks**. They must never be mixed.
166: 
167: | Task type | Who/What | Output |
168: |-----------|---------|--------|
169: | UI Design | Stitch (https://stitch.withgoogle.com/) | Clean JSX, no logic |
170: | Logic | Hook in `hooks/` | Zod, fetch, state |
171: 
172: ### When to Use Stitch
173: 
174: Use Stitch for **every new form or screen**. Required cases:
175: - New page layout
176: - New form (any ERP screen from `docs/frontend/forms/`)
177: - New complex UI block (multi-step wizard, data table, card grid)
178: 
179: ### Stitch Prompt Template
180: 
181: Always include this context when prompting Stitch:
182: 
183: ```
184: Use Tailwind v4 with semantic CSS variables from globals.css.
185: Use shadcn/ui components. Create clean JSX without business logic,
186: fetch calls, or state management.
187: Variables available: bg-primary, text-foreground, bg-muted,
188: text-destructive, border-border, bg-secondary, rounded-xl, etc.
189: ```
190: 
191: ### Implementation Decision
192: 
193: | Stitch result | Action |
194: |---------------|--------|
195: | Matches project color palette and structure | Integrate immediately |
196: | Changes layout structure or adds new elements | Request approval first |
197: | Uses raw Tailwind colors (bg-gray-*, etc.) | Replace with semantic variables before integrating |
198: 
199: ---
200: 
201: ## Frontend TDD Rule
202: 
203: > **Write the test before writing the hook. No exceptions for logic with conditions.**
204: 
205: TDD cycle for every hook with non-trivial logic:
206: 
207: ```
208: 1. Describe scenario (Given/When/Then comment in test file)
209: 2. Write the test  →  hooks/use-xxx.test.ts
210: 3. Present test for approval
211: 4. Implement the hook  →  hooks/use-xxx.ts
212: 5. Confirm: `pnpm test -- use-xxx` passes
213: ```
214: 
215: **Exempt from mandatory tests** (same rule as backend):
216: - Simple data fetching with no conditions or transformations
217: - Static display components
218: 
219: **Requires tests:**
220: - Any hook with `if` / branching logic
221: - Validation hooks (Zod refinements)
222: - Hooks with redirects or role-based behavior
223: - Hooks with timers or side effects
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: ## Navigation Directory
228: 
229: To prevent reading unnecessary context, only look into these files if your current task requires them:
230: 
231: → **Styling, HTTP, UI Components, Icons:** [`conventions.md`](./conventions.md)
232: → **Hooks & Business Logic Rules:** [`hooks.md`](./hooks.md)
233: → **Form Layouts & UI Specs:** [`forms/`](./forms/)
234: → **Form API Logic & Validators:** [`forms-logic.md`](./forms-logic.md)
235: → **Backend SOP (if full-stack feature):** [`docs/backend/sop.md`](../backend/sop.md)
````

## File: docs/other/workflow.md
````markdown
  1: # Git Flow, CI/CD & DevOps
  2: 
  3: ← [Back to Main README](../../README.md)
  4: 
  5: This document is the **official policy** for branching strategy, merge rules, and CI/CD pipeline behavior. All contributors and AI assistants must follow these rules without exception.
  6: 
  7: ---
  8: 
  9: ## 1. Branching Policy
 10: 
 11: ### Branch Map
 12: 
 13: ```
 14: main                          ← production (stable releases only)
 15:   │
 16:   ├── develop-backend         ← all backend feature development
 17:   │     └── feature/backend/<name>
 18:   │
 19:   └── develop-frontend        ← all frontend feature development
 20:         └── feature/frontend/<name>
 21: 
 22: hotfix/<scope>/<name>         ← emergency fix, branched from main
 23: ```
 24: 
 25: ### Rules Per Branch Type
 26: 
 27: #### `main` — Production
 28: - **No direct commits.** Ever.
 29: - Only accepts merges from `release/*` or `hotfix/*` branches.
 30: - Every merge into `main` must be tagged with a version (`vX.Y.Z`).
 31: - High-level configuration changes (e.g., `docker-compose.yml`, `.env.example`, root `README.md`) may be committed directly only if they do not affect application logic.
 32: 
 33: #### `develop-backend` / `develop-frontend` — Active Development
 34: - All feature work happens here.
 35: - Must be kept in a passing state (CI green).
 36: - Direct commits are allowed; prefer short-lived `feature/` branches for larger changes.
 37: 
 38: #### `feature/<scope>/<name>` — Feature Branches
 39: - Branched from the appropriate develop branch.
 40: - Scope values: `backend`, `frontend`, `all`
 41: - Merged back via Pull Request with at least one review.
 42: - Examples: `feature/backend/fefo-algorithm`, `feature/frontend/inventory-dashboard`
 43: 
 44: #### `hotfix/<scope>/<name>` — Emergency Fixes
 45: - **Always branched from `main`**, never from develop.
 46: - After fix is verified:
 47:   1. Merge into `main` (and tag)
 48:   2. Merge into **both** `develop-backend` and `develop-frontend` to keep them in sync
 49: - Examples: `hotfix/backend/jwt-expiry-bug`, `hotfix/all/docker-compose-fix`
 50: 
 51: #### `release/<scope>/<version>` — Release Preparation
 52: - Branched from the appropriate develop branch.
 53: - Only bug fixes allowed — no new features.
 54: - Merged into `main` and back into develop after release.
 55: 
 56: ### Naming Reference
 57: 
 58: | Type | Pattern | Base Branch |
 59: |------|---------|-------------|
 60: | Feature | `feature/<scope>/<name>` | `develop-<scope>` |
 61: | Release | `release/<scope>/<version>` | `develop-<scope>` |
 62: | Hotfix | `hotfix/<scope>/<name>` | `main` |
 63: 
 64: Where `<scope>` is one of: `backend`, `frontend`, `all`.
 65: 
 66: ---
 67: 
 68: ## 2. Merge Rules & Pull Requests
 69: 
 70: - PRs targeting `main` require **at least 1 approval**.
 71: - PRs must pass all CI checks before merge.
 72: - Squash merge is preferred for feature branches to keep `main` history clean.
 73: - Merge commit (no squash) is preferred for release and hotfix branches to preserve audit trail.
 74: 
 75: ---
 76: 
 77: ## 3. Workflow Integrity Rule
 78: 
 79: > **An AI assistant must not modify existing application logic or documentation unless the change directly relates to the task at hand.**
 80: >
 81: > When documentation refactoring is the task: only add links, restructure, and categorize. Do not rewrite technical content.
 82: > When feature development is the task: do not touch unrelated files.
 83: 
 84: ---
 85: 
 86: ## 4. CI/CD Pipeline
 87: 
 88: ### Pre-commit (local)
 89: - **Backend:** `golangci-lint run`
 90: - **Frontend:** `eslint` via `pnpm lint`
 91: 
 92: ### Pull Request Checks
 93: - Backend: `go test ./...` — unit + integration tests
 94: - Frontend: `vitest` — component tests
 95: - Code coverage must not fall below **70%**
 96: 
 97: ### Staging (before release)
 98: - Smoke E2E tests using **Playwright** against the staging environment
 99: - Must pass before any merge into `main`
100: 
101: ### Production Deploy
102: ```bash
103: # From monorepo root
104: docker compose --profile all up -d --build
105: ```
106: 
107: ---
108: 
109: ## 5. DevOps & Infrastructure
110: 
111: ### Services (docker-compose.yml)
112: 
113: | Service | Image | Profile | Port |
114: |---------|-------|---------|------|
115: | `backend` | Custom (Go) | `backend-all`, `all` | `$PORT` |
116: | `frontend` | Custom (Next.js) | `all` | `$FRONTEND_PORT` |
117: | `postgres` | `postgres:17-alpine` | `backend-all`, `all` | `$DB_PORT` |
118: | `valkey` | `valkey/valkey:8-alpine` | `backend-all`, `all` | `$VALKEY_PORT` |
119: | `migrate` | `migrate/migrate` | `backend-all`, `all` | *(runs once)* |
120: | `migrate-down` | `migrate/migrate` | `tools` | *(manual rollback)* |
121: 
122: ### Running Migrations Manually
123: 
124: ```bash
125: # Apply all pending migrations
126: docker compose run --rm migrate
127: 
128: # Roll back last migration
129: docker compose --profile tools run --rm migrate-down
130: ```
131: 
132: ### Environment Variables
133: 
134: Copy `.env.example` → `.env`. Required variables:
135: 
136: | Variable | Required | Description |
137: |----------|----------|-----------|
138: | `DB_HOST` | **Yes** | PostgreSQL host |
139: | `DB_USER` | **Yes** | DB username |
140: | `DB_PASSWORD` | **Yes** | DB password |
141: | `DB_NAME` | **Yes** | Database name |
142: | `JWT_SECRET` | **Yes** | JWT signing secret |
143: | `ADMIN_EMAIL` | **Yes** | Seed admin user email |
144: | `PORT` | No (8080) | Backend HTTP port |
145: | `FRONTEND_PORT` | No | Frontend exposed port |
146: | `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | No | Google OAuth client ID |
147: | `VALKEY_PORT` | No | Valkey port |
````

````typescript
 1: import { ReactNode } from "react";

17:     value,
18:     description,

35:             iconBg: "bg-warning-subtle",
36:             iconColor: "text-warning",
37:             title: "text-warning/70",
38:             value: "text-warning",
39:             desc: "text-warning/80",
40:         },
41:         error: {
42:             wrapper: "bg-card border border-l-4 border-l-destructive shadow-sm",
43:             iconBg: "bg-destructive/10",
44:             iconColor: "text-destructive",
45:             title: "text-destructive/60",
46:             value: "text-destructive",
47:             desc: "text-destructive/80",
48:         },
49:         success: {
50:             wrapper: "bg-card border border-l-4 border-l-success shadow-sm",
51:             iconBg: "bg-success-subtle",
52:             iconColor: "text-success",
53:             title: "text-success/60",
54:             value: "text-success",
55:             desc: "text-success/80",
56:         }
57:     };
58: 
59:     const currentStyle = styles[state];
60: 
61:     return (
62:         <div className={cn("md:col-span-4 p-6 rounded-xl flex flex-col justify-between group transition-all duration-300 cursor-default", currentStyle.wrapper)}>
63:             <div className="flex justify-between items-start mb-4">
64:                 <div className={cn("p-2 rounded-lg", currentStyle.iconBg, currentStyle.iconColor)}>
65:                     {icon}
66:                 </div>
67:                 <span className={cn("text-xs font-bold uppercase tracking-widest", currentStyle.title)}>
68:                     {title}
69:                 </span>
70:             </div>
71:             <div>

## File: frontend/src/app/(auth)/auth/page.tsx
````typescript
 1: "use client"
 2: 
 3: import * as React from "react"
 4: import {
 5:   Card,
 6:   CardContent,
 7:   CardDescription,
 8:   CardHeader,
 9:   CardTitle,
10: } from "@/components/ui/card"
11: import { AuthForm } from "../components/auth-form"
12: import { ApprovalPending } from "../components/approval-pending"
13: import { AuthProvider, useAuth } from "@/hooks/use-auth"
14: 
15: function AuthPageInner() {
16:   const { step } = useAuth()
17: 
18:   if (step === "WAITING") {
19:     return (
20:       <div className="flex items-center justify-center min-h-[400px]">
21:         <ApprovalPending />
22:       </div>
23:     )
24:   }
25: 
26:   const title =
27:     step === "NOT_FOUND" ? "Регистрация" :
28:     step === "VERIFICATION" ? "Введите код" :
29:     "Вход в систему"
30: 
31:   const description =
32:     step === "NOT_FOUND" ? "Создайте новый аккаунт сотрудника" :
33:     step === "VERIFICATION" ? "Проверьте письмо и введите 6-значный код" :
34:     "Введите email для входа или регистрации"
35: 
36:   return (
37:     <div className="w-full max-w-sm animate-in fade-in zoom-in-95 duration-300">
38:       <Card className="rounded-none shadow-lg">
39:         <CardHeader className="pb-3 text-center">
40:           <CardTitle className="text-xl font-semibold tracking-tight">
41:             {title}
42:           </CardTitle>
43:           <CardDescription className="text-xs">
44:             {description}
45:           </CardDescription>
46:         </CardHeader>
47: 
48:         <CardContent className="pt-0">
49:           <AuthForm />
50:         </CardContent>
51:       </Card>
52: 
53:       <p className="mt-3 text-center text-[10px] text-muted-foreground">
54:         Нажимая «Продолжить», вы принимаете{" "}
55:         <a
56:           href="#"
57:           className="underline underline-offset-2 hover:text-foreground transition-colors"
58:         >
59:           условия использования
60:         </a>
61:         .
62:       </p>
63:     </div>
64:   )
65: }
66: 
67: export default function AuthPage() {
68:   return (
69:     <AuthProvider>
70:       <AuthPageInner />
71:     </AuthProvider>
72:   )
73: }
````

18:           "border-border text-foreground [a]:hover:bg-muted [a]:hover:text-muted-foreground",
19:         ghost:
20:           "hover:bg-muted hover:text-muted-foreground dark:hover:bg-muted/50",
21:         link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
22:       },
23:     },
24:     defaultVariants: {
25:       variant: "default",
26:     },
27:   }
28: )
29: 
30: function Badge({
31:   className,
32:   variant = "default",
33:   asChild = false,
34:   ...props
35: }: React.ComponentProps<"span"> &
36:   VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> & { asChild?: boolean }) {
37:   const Comp = asChild ? Slot.Root : "span"
38: 
39:   return (
40:     <Comp
41:       data-slot="badge"
42:       data-variant={variant}
43:       className={cn(badgeVariants({ variant }), className)}
44:       {...props}
45:     />
46:   )
47: }
48: 
49: export { Badge, badgeVariants }
````

## File: frontend/src/components/ui/button.tsx

 6: 
 7: const buttonVariants = cva(
 8:   "group/button inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center rounded-none border border-transparent bg-clip-padding text-xs font-medium whitespace-nowrap transition-all outline-none select-none focus-visible:border-ring focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring/50 active:scale-[0.98] active:translate-y-px disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 aria-invalid:border-destructive aria-invalid:ring-1 aria-invalid:ring-destructive/20 dark:aria-invalid:border-destructive/50 dark:aria-invalid:ring-destructive/40 [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg]:shrink-0 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
 9:   {
10:     variants: {
11:       variant: {
12:         default: "bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/85 active:bg-primary/95",
13:         outline:
14:           "border-border bg-background hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground aria-expanded:bg-muted aria-expanded:text-foreground dark:border-input dark:bg-input/30 dark:hover:bg-input/50",
15:         secondary:
16:           "bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80 aria-expanded:bg-secondary aria-expanded:text-secondary-foreground",
17:         ghost:
18:           "hover:bg-muted hover:text-foreground aria-expanded:bg-muted aria-expanded:text-foreground dark:hover:bg-muted/50",
19:         destructive:
20:           "bg-destructive/10 text-destructive hover:bg-destructive/20 focus-visible:border-destructive/40 focus-visible:ring-destructive/20 dark:bg-destructive/20 dark:hover:bg-destructive/30 dark:focus-visible:ring-destructive/40",
21:         link: "text-primary underline-offset-4 hover:underline",
22:       },
23:       size: {
24:         default:
25:           "h-8 gap-1.5 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
26:         xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-none px-2 text-xs has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
27:         sm: "h-7 gap-1 rounded-none px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3.5",
28:         lg: "h-9 gap-1.5 px-2.5 has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-2 has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-2",
29:         icon: "size-8",
30:         "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3",
31:         "icon-sm": "size-7 rounded-none",
32:         "icon-lg": "size-9",
33:       },
34:     },
35:     defaultVariants: {
36:       variant: "default",
37:       size: "default",
38:     },
39:   }
40: )
41: 
42: function Button({
43:   className,
44:   variant = "default",
45:   size = "default",
46:   asChild = false,
47:   ...props
48: }: React.ComponentProps<"button"> &
49:   VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
50:     asChild?: boolean
51:   }) {

53:

## File: frontend/src/components/ui/calendar.tsx
````typescript
  1: "use client"
  2: 
  3: import * as React from "react"
  4: import {
  5:   DayPicker,
  6:   getDefaultClassNames,
  7:   type DayButton,
  8:   type Locale,
  9: } from "react-day-picker"
 10: 
 11: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
 12: import { Button, buttonVariants } from "@/components/ui/button"
 13: import { CaretLeftIcon, CaretRightIcon, CaretDownIcon } from "@phosphor-icons/react"
 14: 
 15: function Calendar({
 16:   className,
 17:   classNames,
 18:   showOutsideDays = true,
 19:   captionLayout = "label",
 20:   buttonVariant = "ghost",
 21:   locale,
 22:   formatters,
 23:   components,
 24:   ...props
 25: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof DayPicker> & {
 26:   buttonVariant?: React.ComponentProps<typeof Button>["variant"]
 27: }) {
 28:   const defaultClassNames = getDefaultClassNames()
 29: 
 30:   return (
 31:     <DayPicker
 32:       showOutsideDays={showOutsideDays}
 33:       className={cn(
 34:         "group/calendar bg-background p-2 [--cell-size:--spacing(7)] in-data-[slot=card-content]:bg-transparent in-data-[slot=popover-content]:bg-transparent",
 35:         String.raw`rtl:**:[.rdp-button\_next>svg]:rotate-180`,
 36:         String.raw`rtl:**:[.rdp-button\_previous>svg]:rotate-180`,
 37:         className
 38:       )}
 39:       captionLayout={captionLayout}
 40:       locale={locale}
 41:       formatters={{
 42:         formatMonthDropdown: (date) =>
 43:           date.toLocaleString(locale?.code, { month: "short" }),
 44:         ...formatters,
 45:       }}
 46:       classNames={{
 47:         root: cn("w-fit", defaultClassNames.root),
 48:         months: cn(
 49:           "relative flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row",
 50:           defaultClassNames.months
 51:         ),
 52:         month: cn("flex w-full flex-col gap-4", defaultClassNames.month),
 53:         nav: cn(
 54:           "absolute inset-x-0 top-0 flex w-full items-center justify-between gap-1",
 55:           defaultClassNames.nav
 56:         ),
 57:         button_previous: cn(
 58:           buttonVariants({ variant: buttonVariant }),
 59:           "size-(--cell-size) p-0 select-none aria-disabled:opacity-50",
 60:           defaultClassNames.button_previous
 61:         ),
 62:         button_next: cn(
 63:           buttonVariants({ variant: buttonVariant }),
 64:           "size-(--cell-size) p-0 select-none aria-disabled:opacity-50",
 65:           defaultClassNames.button_next
 66:         ),
 67:         month_caption: cn(
 68:           "flex h-(--cell-size) w-full items-center justify-center px-(--cell-size)",
 69:           defaultClassNames.month_caption
 70:         ),
 71:         dropdowns: cn(
 72:           "flex h-(--cell-size) w-full items-center justify-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-medium",
 73:           defaultClassNames.dropdowns
 74:         ),
 75:         dropdown_root: cn(
 76:           "relative rounded-(--cell-radius)",
 77:           defaultClassNames.dropdown_root
 78:         ),
 79:         dropdown: cn(
 80:           "absolute inset-0 bg-popover opacity-0",
 81:           defaultClassNames.dropdown
 82:         ),
 83:         caption_label: cn(
 84:           "font-medium select-none",
 85:           captionLayout === "label"
 86:             ? "text-sm"
 87:             : "flex items-center gap-1 rounded-(--cell-radius) text-sm [&>svg]:size-3.5 [&>svg]:text-muted-foreground",
 88:           defaultClassNames.caption_label
 89:         ),
 90:         table: "w-full border-collapse",
 91:         weekdays: cn("flex", defaultClassNames.weekdays),
 92:         weekday: cn(
 93:           "flex-1 rounded-(--cell-radius) text-[0.8rem] font-normal text-muted-foreground select-none",
 94:           defaultClassNames.weekday
 95:         ),
 96:         week: cn("mt-2 flex w-full", defaultClassNames.week),
 97:         week_number_header: cn(
 98:           "w-(--cell-size) select-none",
 99:           defaultClassNames.week_number_header
100:         ),
101:         week_number: cn(
102:           "text-[0.8rem] text-muted-foreground select-none",
103:           defaultClassNames.week_number
104:         ),
105:         day: cn(
106:           "group/day relative aspect-square h-full w-full rounded-(--cell-radius) p-0 text-center select-none [&:last-child[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-r-(--cell-radius)",
107:           props.showWeekNumber
108:             ? "[&:nth-child(2)[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-l-(--cell-radius)"
109:             : "[&:first-child[data-selected=true]_button]:rounded-l-(--cell-radius)",
110:           defaultClassNames.day
111:         ),
112:         range_start: cn(
113:           "relative isolate z-0 rounded-l-(--cell-radius) bg-muted after:absolute after:inset-y-0 after:right-0 after:w-4 after:bg-muted",
114:           defaultClassNames.range_start
115:         ),
116:         range_middle: cn("rounded-none", defaultClassNames.range_middle),
117:         range_end: cn(
118:           "relative isolate z-0 rounded-r-(--cell-radius) bg-muted after:absolute after:inset-y-0 after:left-0 after:w-4 after:bg-muted",
119:           defaultClassNames.range_end
120:         ),
121:         today: cn(
122:           "rounded-(--cell-radius) bg-muted text-foreground data-[selected=true]:rounded-none",
123:           defaultClassNames.today
124:         ),
125:         outside: cn(
126:           "text-muted-foreground aria-selected:text-muted-foreground",
127:           defaultClassNames.outside
128:         ),
129:         disabled: cn(
130:           "text-muted-foreground opacity-50",
131:           defaultClassNames.disabled
132:         ),
133:         hidden: cn("invisible", defaultClassNames.hidden),
134:         ...classNames,
135:       }}
136:       components={{
137:         Root: ({ className, rootRef, ...props }) => {
138:           return (
139:             <div
140:               data-slot="calendar"
141:               ref={rootRef}
142:               className={cn(className)}
143:               {...props}
144:             />
145:           )
146:         },
147:         Chevron: ({ className, orientation, ...props }) => {
148:           if (orientation === "left") {
149:             return (
150:               <CaretLeftIcon className={cn("size-4", className)} {...props} />
151:             )
152:           }
153: 
154:           if (orientation === "right") {
155:             return (
156:               <CaretRightIcon className={cn("size-4", className)} {...props} />
157:             )
158:           }
159: 
160:           return (
161:             <CaretDownIcon className={cn("size-4", className)} {...props} />
162:           )
163:         },
164:         DayButton: ({ ...props }) => (
165:           <CalendarDayButton locale={locale} {...props} />
166:         ),
167:         WeekNumber: ({ children, ...props }) => {
168:           return (
169:             <td {...props}>
170:               <div className="flex size-(--cell-size) items-center justify-center text-center">
171:                 {children}
172:               </div>
173:             </td>
174:           )
175:         },
176:         ...components,
177:       }}
178:       {...props}
179:     />
180:   )
181: }
182: 
183: function CalendarDayButton({
184:   className,
185:   day,
186:   modifiers,
187:   locale,
188:   ...props
189: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof DayButton> & { locale?: Partial<Locale> }) {
190:   const defaultClassNames = getDefaultClassNames()
191: 
192:   const ref = React.useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null)
193:   React.useEffect(() => {
194:     if (modifiers.focused) ref.current?.focus()
195:   }, [modifiers.focused])
196: 
197:   return (
198:     <Button
199:       ref={ref}
200:       variant="ghost"
201:       size="icon"
202:       data-day={day.date.toLocaleDateString(locale?.code)}
203:       data-selected-single={
204:         modifiers.selected &&
205:         !modifiers.range_start &&
206:         !modifiers.range_end &&
207:         !modifiers.range_middle
208:       }
209:       data-range-start={modifiers.range_start}
210:       data-range-end={modifiers.range_end}
211:       data-range-middle={modifiers.range_middle}
212:       className={cn(
213:         "relative isolate z-10 flex aspect-square size-auto w-full min-w-(--cell-size) flex-col gap-1 border-0 leading-none font-normal group-data-[focused=true]/day:relative group-data-[focused=true]/day:z-10 group-data-[focused=true]/day:border-ring group-data-[focused=true]/day:ring-[3px] group-data-[focused=true]/day:ring-ring/50 data-[range-end=true]:rounded-(--cell-radius) data-[range-end=true]:rounded-r-(--cell-radius) data-[range-end=true]:bg-primary data-[range-end=true]:text-primary-foreground data-[range-middle=true]:rounded-none data-[range-middle=true]:bg-muted data-[range-middle=true]:text-foreground data-[range-start=true]:rounded-(--cell-radius) data-[range-start=true]:rounded-l-(--cell-radius) data-[range-start=true]:bg-primary data-[range-start=true]:text-primary-foreground data-[selected-single=true]:bg-primary data-[selected-single=true]:text-primary-foreground dark:hover:text-foreground [&>span]:text-xs [&>span]:opacity-70",
214:         defaultClassNames.day,
215:         className
216:       )}
217:       {...props}
218:     />
219:   )
220: }
221: 
222: export { Calendar, CalendarDayButton }
````

 52:         <DialogTitle>{title}</DialogTitle>
 53:         <DialogDescription>{description}</DialogDescription>
 54:       </DialogHeader>
 55:       <DialogContent

 58:           className
 59:         )}
 60:         showCloseButton={showCloseButton}
 61:       >
 62:         {children}
 63:       </DialogContent>
 64:     </Dialog>
 65:   )
 66: }

102:       {...props}

129:         className

159:         className
160:       )}

162:     >
163:       {children}

165:     </CommandPrimitive.Item>

178:         className
179:       )}

181:     />
182:   )
183: }

 32: }

 41:       className={cn(
 42:         "fixed inset-0 isolate z-50 bg-black/10 duration-100 supports-backdrop-filter:backdrop-blur-xs data-open:animate-in data-open:fade-in-0 data-closed:animate-out data-closed:fade-out-0",
 43:         className

 55: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof DialogPrimitive.Content> & {

 57: }) {

 60:       <DialogOverlay />

 62:         data-slot="dialog-content"
 63:         className={cn(
 64:           "fixed top-1/2 left-1/2 z-50 grid w-full max-w-[calc(100%-2rem)] -translate-x-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 gap-4 rounded-none bg-popover p-4 text-xs/relaxed text-popover-foreground ring-1 ring-foreground/10 duration-100 outline-none sm:max-w-sm data-open:animate-in data-open:fade-in-0 data-open:zoom-in-95 data-closed:animate-out data-closed:fade-out-0 data-closed:zoom-out-95",
 65:           className
 66:         )}
 67:         {...props}
 68:       >
 69:         {children}
 70:         {showCloseButton && (
 71:           <DialogPrimitive.Close data-slot="dialog-close" asChild>
 72:             <Button
 73:               variant="ghost"
 74:               className="absolute top-2 right-2"
 75:               size="icon-sm"
 76:             >
 77:               <XIcon
 78:               />
 79:               <span className="sr-only">Close</span>
 80:             </Button>
 81:           </DialogPrimitive.Close>
 82:         )}
 83:       </DialogPrimitive.Content>
 84:     </DialogPortal>
 85:   )
 86: }
 87:

 91:       data-slot="dialog-header"

 93:       {...props}
 94:     />
 95:   )
 96: }

105: }) {

111:         className
112:       )}
113:       {...props}
114:     >

119:         </DialogPrimitive.Close>
120:       )}
121:     </div>
122:   )

124: 
125: function DialogTitle({
126:   className,
127:   ...props
128: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof DialogPrimitive.Title>) {
129:   return (
130:     <DialogPrimitive.Title
131:       data-slot="dialog-title"

133:       {...props}
134:     />

141: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof DialogPrimitive.Description>) {
142:   return (

144:       data-slot="dialog-description"

147:         className
148:       )}
149:       {...props}
150:     />
151:   )
152: }
153:

 37:           "order-last w-full justify-start px-2.5 pb-2 group-has-[>input]/input-group:pb-2 [.border-t]:pt-2",
 38:       },
 39:     },
 40:     defaultVariants: {
 41:       align: "inline-start",
 42:     },
 43:   }
 44: )
 45: 
 46: function InputGroupAddon({
 47:   className,
 48:   align = "inline-start",
 49:   ...props
 50: }: React.ComponentProps<"div"> & VariantProps<typeof inputGroupAddonVariants>) {
 51:   return (
 52:     <div
 53:       role="group"
 54:       data-slot="input-group-addon"
 55:       data-align={align}
 56:       className={cn(inputGroupAddonVariants({ align }), className)}
 57:       onClick={(e) => {
 58:         if ((e.target as HTMLElement).closest("button")) {
 59:           return
 60:         }
 61:         e.currentTarget.parentElement?.querySelector("input")?.focus()
 62:       }}
 63:       {...props}
 64:     />
 65:   )
 66: }
 67: 
 68: const inputGroupButtonVariants = cva(
 69:   "flex items-center gap-2 text-xs shadow-none",
 70:   {
 71:     variants: {
 72:       size: {
 73:         xs: "h-6 gap-1 rounded-none px-1.5 [&>svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-3.5",
 74:         sm: "gap-1",
 75:         "icon-xs": "size-6 rounded-none p-0 has-[>svg]:p-0",
 76:         "icon-sm": "size-7 p-0 has-[>svg]:p-0",
 77:       },
 78:     },
 79:     defaultVariants: {
 80:       size: "xs",
 81:     },
 82:   }
 83: )
 84: 
 85: function InputGroupButton({
 86:   className,
 87:   type = "button",
 88:   variant = "ghost",
 89:   size = "xs",
 90:   ...props
 91: }: Omit<React.ComponentProps<typeof Button>, "size"> &
 92:   VariantProps<typeof inputGroupButtonVariants>) {
 93:   return (
 94:     <Button
 95:       type={type}
 96:       data-size={size}
 97:       variant={variant}
 98:       className={cn(inputGroupButtonVariants({ size }), className)}
 99:       {...props}
100:     />
101:   )
102: }
103: 
104: function InputGroupText({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"span">) {
105:   return (
106:     <span
107:       className={cn(
108:         "flex items-center gap-2 text-xs text-muted-foreground [&_svg]:pointer-events-none [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
109:         className
110:       )}
111:       {...props}
112:     />
113:   )
114: }
115: 
116: function InputGroupInput({
117:   className,
118:   ...props
119: }: React.ComponentProps<"input">) {
120:   return (
121:     <Input
122:       data-slot="input-group-control"
123:       className={cn(
124:         "flex-1 rounded-none border-0 bg-transparent shadow-none ring-0 focus-visible:ring-0 disabled:bg-transparent aria-invalid:ring-0 dark:bg-transparent dark:disabled:bg-transparent",
125:         className
126:       )}
127:       {...props}
128:     />
129:   )
130: }
131: 
132: function InputGroupTextarea({
133:   className,
134:   ...props
135: }: React.ComponentProps<"textarea">) {
136:   return (
137:     <Textarea
138:       data-slot="input-group-control"
139:       className={cn(
140:         "flex-1 resize-none rounded-none border-0 bg-transparent py-2 shadow-none ring-0 focus-visible:ring-0 disabled:bg-transparent aria-invalid:ring-0 dark:bg-transparent dark:disabled:bg-transparent",
141:         className
142:       )}
143:       {...props}
144:     />
145:   )
146: }
147: 
148: export {
149:   InputGroup,
150:   InputGroupAddon,
151:   InputGroupButton,
152:   InputGroupText,
153:   InputGroupInput,
154:   InputGroupTextarea,
155: }
````

````typescript
  1: import * as React from "react"
  2: 
  3: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
  4: import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"
  5: import { CaretLeftIcon, CaretRightIcon, DotsThreeIcon } from "@phosphor-icons/react"
  6: 
  7: function Pagination({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"nav">) {
  8:   return (
  9:     <nav
 10:       role="navigation"
 11:       aria-label="pagination"
 12:       data-slot="pagination"
 13:       className={cn("mx-auto flex w-full justify-center", className)}
 14:       {...props}
 15:     />
 16:   )
 17: }
 18: 
 19: function PaginationContent({
 20:   className,
 21:   ...props
 22: }: React.ComponentProps<"ul">) {
 23:   return (
 24:     <ul
 25:       data-slot="pagination-content"
 26:       className={cn("flex items-center gap-0.5", className)}
 27:       {...props}
 28:     />
 29:   )
 30: }
 31: 
 32: function PaginationItem({ ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"li">) {
 33:   return <li data-slot="pagination-item" {...props} />
 34: }
 35: 
 36: type PaginationLinkProps = {
 37:   isActive?: boolean
 38: } & Pick<React.ComponentProps<typeof Button>, "size"> &
 39:   React.ComponentProps<"a">
 40: 
 41: function PaginationLink({
 42:   className,
 43:   isActive,
 44:   size = "icon",
 45:   ...props
 46: }: PaginationLinkProps) {
 47:   return (
 48:     <Button
 49:       asChild
 50:       variant={isActive ? "outline" : "ghost"}
 51:       size={size}
 52:       className={cn(className)}
 53:     >
 54:       <a
 55:         aria-current={isActive ? "page" : undefined}
 56:         data-slot="pagination-link"
 57:         data-active={isActive}
 58:         {...props}
 59:       />
 60:     </Button>
 61:   )
 62: }
 63: 
 64: function PaginationPrevious({
 65:   className,
 66:   text = "Previous",
 67:   ...props
 68: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof PaginationLink> & { text?: string }) {
 69:   return (
 70:     <PaginationLink
 71:       aria-label="Go to previous page"
 72:       size="default"
 73:       className={cn("pl-1.5!", className)}
 74:       {...props}
 75:     >
 76:       <CaretLeftIcon data-icon="inline-start" />
 77:       <span className="hidden sm:block">{text}</span>
 78:     </PaginationLink>
 79:   )
 80: }
 81: 
 82: function PaginationNext({
 83:   className,
 84:   text = "Next",
 85:   ...props
 86: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof PaginationLink> & { text?: string }) {
 87:   return (
 88:     <PaginationLink
 89:       aria-label="Go to next page"
 90:       size="default"
 91:       className={cn("pr-1.5!", className)}
 92:       {...props}
 93:     >
 94:       <span className="hidden sm:block">{text}</span>
 95:       <CaretRightIcon data-icon="inline-end" />
 96:     </PaginationLink>
 97:   )
 98: }
 99: 
100: function PaginationEllipsis({
101:   className,
102:   ...props
103: }: React.ComponentProps<"span">) {
104:   return (
105:     <span
106:       aria-hidden
107:       data-slot="pagination-ellipsis"
108:       className={cn(
109:         "flex size-8 items-center justify-center [&_svg:not([class*='size-'])]:size-4",
110:         className
111:       )}
112:       {...props}
113:     >
114:       <DotsThreeIcon
115:       />
116:       <span className="sr-only">More pages</span>
117:     </span>
118:   )
119: }
120: 
121: export {
122:   Pagination,
123:   PaginationContent,
124:   PaginationEllipsis,
125:   PaginationItem,
126:   PaginationLink,

 97: }: React.ComponentProps<"caption">) {
 98:   return (
 99:     <caption
100:       data-slot="table-caption"
101:       className={cn("mt-4 text-xs text-muted-foreground", className)}
102:       {...props}
103:     />
104:   )
105: }
106: 
107: export {
108:   Table,
109:   TableHeader,
110:   TableBody,
111:   TableFooter,
112:   TableHead,
113:   TableRow,
114:   TableCell,
115:   TableCaption,
116: }
````

## File: frontend/src/components/ui/toggle-group.tsx
````typescript
 1: "use client"
 2: 
 3: import * as React from "react"
 4: import { type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"
 5: import { ToggleGroup as ToggleGroupPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
 6: 
 7: import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"
 8: import { toggleVariants } from "@/components/ui/toggle"
 9: 
10: const ToggleGroupContext = React.createContext<
11:   VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
12:     spacing?: number
13:     orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
14:   }
15: >({
16:   size: "default",
17:   variant: "default",
18:   spacing: 0,
19:   orientation: "horizontal",
20: })
21: 
22: function ToggleGroup({
23:   className,
24:   variant,
25:   size,
26:   spacing = 0,
27:   orientation = "horizontal",
28:   children,
29:   ...props
30: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root> &
31:   VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants> & {
32:     spacing?: number
33:     orientation?: "horizontal" | "vertical"
34:   }) {
35:   return (
36:     <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root
37:       data-slot="toggle-group"
38:       data-variant={variant}
39:       data-size={size}
40:       data-spacing={spacing}
41:       data-orientation={orientation}
42:       style={{ "--gap": spacing } as React.CSSProperties}
43:       className={cn(
44:         "group/toggle-group flex w-fit flex-row items-center gap-[--spacing(var(--gap))] rounded-none data-[size=sm]:rounded-none data-vertical:flex-col data-vertical:items-stretch",
45:         className
46:       )}
47:       {...props}
48:     >
49:       <ToggleGroupContext.Provider
50:         value={{ variant, size, spacing, orientation }}
51:       >
52:         {children}
53:       </ToggleGroupContext.Provider>
54:     </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Root>
55:   )
56: }
57: 
58: function ToggleGroupItem({
59:   className,
60:   children,
61:   variant = "default",
62:   size = "default",
63:   ...props
64: }: React.ComponentProps<typeof ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item> &
65:   VariantProps<typeof toggleVariants>) {
66:   const context = React.useContext(ToggleGroupContext)
67: 
68:   return (
69:     <ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item
70:       data-slot="toggle-group-item"
71:       data-variant={context.variant || variant}
72:       data-size={context.size || size}
73:       data-spacing={context.spacing}
74:       className={cn(
75:         "shrink-0 group-data-[spacing=0]/toggle-group:rounded-none group-data-[spacing=0]/toggle-group:px-2 focus:z-10 focus-visible:z-10 group-data-[spacing=0]/toggle-group:has-data-[icon=inline-end]:pr-1.5 group-data-[spacing=0]/toggle-group:has-data-[icon=inline-start]:pl-1.5 group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-none group-data-vertical/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:first:rounded-none group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-none group-data-vertical/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:last:rounded-none group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-l-0 group-data-vertical/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:border-t-0 group-data-horizontal/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-l group-data-vertical/toggle-group:data-[spacing=0]:data-[variant=outline]:first:border-t",
76:         toggleVariants({
77:           variant: context.variant || variant,
78:           size: context.size || size,
79:         }),
80:         className
81:       )}
82:       {...props}
83:     >
84:       {children}
85:     </ToggleGroupPrimitive.Item>
86:   )
87: }
88: 
89: export { ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem }
````
